REGIONS.name: Into Labrynna
REGIONS.sub: Main Quest — Lynna & the Maku Tree
REGIONS.tagline: An oracle possessed, history unmade, and a harp that turns back time.

steps.t: The Triforce carries Link to the land of Labrynna. You meet Impa, searching for a singer — and then Nayru, the Oracle of Ages, whose song calms the very land.
The Sorceress of Shadows, Veran, possesses Nayru's body and flees into the PAST to rewrite history, withering the present. The Maku Tree of the present begins to die.
Reach the Maku Tree (you'll travel to the PAST to find her healthy). She tells you to gather the eight Essences of Time from Labrynna's dungeons to restore her power and stop Veran.
steps.stuck: Follow the story to a time portal and cross into the past; the Maku Tree there is strong and gives your quest. The present-day tree is too weak to help yet.

steps.t: Find your first sword near Lynna — open the chest in the starting cave for the Wooden Sword.

items.name: Wooden Sword
items.note: Your starting blade. Slash, and hold to charge a Spin Attack. Later upgraded to the Noble Sword and Master Sword.

steps.stuck: The sword is in a small cave near Lynna Village / Nayru's house area. Go in and open the chest.

steps.t: Obtain the Harp of Ages, the instrument that lets you cross between the past and present. You learn the Tune of Echoes, which opens time portals.

items.name: Harp of Ages
items.note: Play its tunes to travel between the PAST and PRESENT. The Tune of Echoes opens portals; you learn the Tune of Currents and Tune of Ages later.

steps.stuck: The Harp comes from Nayru / the early story. Once you have it and the Tune of Echoes, play it on a glowing time-portal tile to switch eras.

steps.t: Get used to time travel. A change you make in the PAST shows up in the PRESENT: dam a river long ago and a lake runs dry today; move a block in one era to clear a path in the other. When stuck, flip time and look at the same spot.
Head to Spirit's Grave, the first of the eight dungeons, in the graveyard area. Use the Harp to reach its entrance across eras, grabbing seed trees and Gasha spots along the way.
steps.stuck: Spirit's Grave is in the Yoll Graveyard. You'll likely cross to the past (or back) at a nearby portal to reach the dungeon door.

steps.t: Your quest is set: clear all eight dungeons, claim the eight Essences of Time, wake the Maku Seed, and confront Veran to free Nayru and Labrynna.

REGIONS.name: Spirit's Grave
REGIONS.tagline: The first dungeon, hidden beneath a grave in Yoll Graveyard.

sections.name: Yoll Graveyard
sections.sub: Find the way into the grave

steps.t: From Lynna, head east out of town and work your way to Yoll Graveyard. Small trees and bushes block the path — you'll need fire to clear them.
steps.stuck: From the area east of town, follow the path east and south toward the graveyard. Burn the bushes in your way by tossing an Ember Seed at them.

steps.t: The Maku Tree gives you the Seed Satchel and Ember Seeds — they light torches and burn the little trees and bushes blocking the graveyard.

items.name: Ember Seeds
items.note: Burn small trees/bushes and light torches.

steps.t: Inside the graveyard you'll overhear children talking about a ghost living under a grave beneath a tree. That grave is your way into the dungeon — remember it.
Work your way to the grave at the south end and head down the stairs. In the cave below, light the torches with Ember Seeds to make the Graveyard Key drop, then grab it.
steps.stuck: Toss an Ember Seed at each unlit torch in the room. Once they're lit, the Graveyard Key appears in the middle of the room — walk over and pick it up.

items.name: Graveyard Key
items.note: Unlocks the gate up to the ghost's grave.

steps.t: Back outside, make your way to the locked gate and use the Graveyard Key to open it, opening the path to the grave beneath the tree.
steps.stuck: Head up toward the locked gate — walk into the lock with the Graveyard Key in your inventory to open it.

steps.t: Talk to the ghost at the tree-covered grave. Push the gravestone aside to open a hole, then drop in — this is the entrance to Spirit's Grave.
steps.stuck: Speak to the ghost first, then push the gravestone (it slides) and step into the opening it reveals.

steps.t: Inside, fight through the rooms, lighting torches with Ember Seeds and using keys on locked doors as you find them. Push blocks and step on floor switches to open the way deeper.
Find the dungeon's treasure chest: the Power Bracelet. Equip it to a button — now you can lift and throw pots and rocks, and pull heavy blocks.
steps.stuck: It's in a chest reached by going down a ladder and up another. Stand next to a heavy object and press the Bracelet button to grab it; hold and pull to drag blocks.

items.name: Power Bracelet
items.note: Lift pots and rocks; required to beat the boss.

steps.t: With the Power Bracelet, you can now clear rooms that were blocked by heavy stones — lift the rock or pot in your way and toss it aside to move on.
Mini-boss: Giant Ghini. It sends out small ghosts that swarm you — slash those away, then close in and hit the Giant Ghini with your sword.
steps.stuck: Cut down the little ghosts it spawns so you can move freely, then sword the big Ghini. It takes several hits; clear it and a key drops to continue.

steps.t: Boss: Pumpkin Head. Slash its body until it vanishes and the pumpkin head drops to the floor. Lift the pumpkin with the Power Bracelet — a little ghost is hiding inside.
steps.stuck: Hack at the body with your sword until it disappears, then use the Power Bracelet to pick the fallen pumpkin up off the ground, uncovering the ghost.

steps.t: While you're holding the pumpkin, throw it far from the ghost, then slash the exposed ghost before it reforms. Repeat: slash body → lift and throw pumpkin → hit the ghost until it's beaten.
steps.stuck: The ghost is only vulnerable while the pumpkin is away from it — throw the pumpkin as far as you can, then strike the ghost fast before it grabs the pumpkin and re-forms.

steps.t: After Pumpkin Head falls, grab the Heart Container it leaves in the center of the room — one more permanent heart on your life bar.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: +1 permanent heart.

steps.t: Head up through the north exit and step onto the altar to claim the first Essence of Time: the Eternal Spirit. Return toward the Maku Tree to keep waking her.

items.name: Eternal Spirit
items.note: Essence of Time 1 of 8.

REGIONS.name: Wing Dungeon
REGIONS.tagline: A feather to leap with, in the western woods.

sections.name: Into the Fairies' Woods
sections.sub: Catch the fairies, then cross to the past

steps.t: Head west out of town into the Western Woods, the Fairies' Woods. Three Stray Fairies pop out of a tree and warp the forest into a looping maze for a game of hide-and-seek. You can't reach the dungeon until you find all three.

Find the three hidden fairies to break the maze: one is under a rock (lift it with the Power Bracelet), the others hide in bushes you slash. Walk the screens back and forth until each hiding spot appears, then grab the fairy.
steps.stuck: The looping exits are the catch — until all three fairies are found, heading east just loops you back. From the start: go up/down/up and lift the rock for one; slash the bottom bushes after stepping right for the others. Catch all three and the forest stops warping.

steps.t: Walk to the dungeon, but it collapses the moment you reach it — you can't enter here in the present. The Maku Tree sends you to Nayru's home; grab the Harp of Ages from the hidden cellar, learn the Tune of Echoes, then play it on a time portal to drop into the PAST, where this forest is the Deku Forest.
steps.stuck: The Wing Dungeon only exists intact in the PAST. If there's no door, you're in the present — get the Harp of Ages from Nayru's house, then play the Tune of Echoes on a portal to warp back in time.

steps.t: In the past, find the building in the Deku Forest. Bomb the entrance to open it, then step inside the Wing Dungeon.
steps.stuck: The dungeon entrance in the past is sealed. Set a Bomb right against it, back off, and the blast blows it open — then walk in.

steps.t: Push through the early rooms to the mini-boss, Swoop — a giant bird that dive-bombs wherever you're standing, smashing the floor tiles there. Keep moving so it misses, then slash it the instant it lands.
steps.stuck: Stand on the BLUE border tiles around the room's edge — Swoop can't break those. Wait for it to crash down, then run in and hit it with your sword as it sits stunned. A few good hits ends it; later it bounces toward you, so dodge before you strike.

steps.t: Past Swoop is a side-scrolling room with a Thwomp. Bait the Thwomp into slamming down, then dash underneath as it rises and grab Roc's Feather on the right.
steps.stuck: Step in so the Thwomp drops, wait, and run past it the moment it starts going back up. The chest beyond holds Roc's Feather — equip it and press the button to JUMP.

items.name: Roc's Feather
items.note: Equip it to jump over gaps, hazards, and tiles.

steps.t: Roc's Feather changes everything from here on: equip it to leap over pits, spikes, and floor tiles. Many remaining doors only open once you jump across the right tiles.
Use the Feather to clear the locked rooms — jump on the colored tiles to flip them and across gaps until each gate unlocks, working your way toward the boss door. Pick up the Boss Key along the way.
steps.stuck: When a room has a closed gate and colored floor tiles, jump on every tile to change its color (don't leave any) — clearing the set drops the gate. Use the Feather to cross the pits between them.

steps.t: In the boss room, climb to the ledges at the top. Head Thwomp sits in the middle, slowly rotating to show four colored faces — and spitting fireballs from whichever face is forward.
Watch its fireballs and reposition along the top ledges to stay out of the line of fire. Keep Bombs and Roc's Feather equipped — you'll need the Feather to climb back up if you fall.
The top of its head is open. From a ledge, drop a Bomb straight into the hole — but only the RED face takes real damage. Time it so the Bomb lands inside just as the red face comes around.
steps.stuck: Hitting any other color does nothing. Wait until it rotates to the RED face, then toss a Bomb into the open top of its head. About four clean hits destroys it.

steps.t: With Head Thwomp beaten, claim the Heart Container, then step into the light to take the second Essence of Time — the Ancient Wood.

items.name: Ancient Wood
items.note: The 2nd Essence of Time. Two down; carry it back toward the Maku Tree.

REGIONS.name: Moonlit Grotto
REGIONS.tagline: A seed shooter that bounces off the walls.

sections.name: Sailing to Crescent Island
sections.sub: Build a raft, then warp to the statue's mouth

steps.t: Crescent Island sits out at sea — you can't walk there. Find Rafton's hut south of Lynna Village in the PAST; he'll build you a raft, but he needs a rope that won't rot in seawater first.
steps.stuck: Rafton lives in the PAST. If his hut looks empty in the present, play the Harp of Ages at a time portal and come back in the past era.

steps.t: Bring Rafton the Cheval Rope. You get it (along with the Zora's Flippers) from Cheval's Tomb in the Yoll Graveyard. Hand him the rope and he starts building.

items.name: Cheval Rope
items.note: A rope that doesn't rot in seawater. Rafton needs it to finish your raft.

steps.stuck: No rope yet? It's inside Cheval's Tomb in Yoll Graveyard — the same place you collect the Zora's Flippers. Clear that first.

steps.t: Rafton also wants a sea chart so the raft can be steered. Track down Tingle and get the Island Chart, then return to Rafton — the raft is ready.

items.name: Island Chart
items.note: A sea map. Give it to Rafton so he can finish the raft to Crescent Island.

steps.stuck: Tingle is the floating map-seller in the Forest of Time. Reach him to receive the Island Chart, then take it back to Rafton.

steps.t: Ride the raft out to sea. A storm hits and you wash up on Crescent Island — and the lizard-like Tokay swarm you and STEAL all your items. You start with nothing but a shovel.
steps.stuck: Don't panic when your gear's gone — this is scripted. Everything is recoverable on the island.

steps.t: Get your gear back: at the Tokay's trading hut, trade up from the shovel — swapping items one at a time — until you've reclaimed your sword, bombs, feather, flippers, seed satchel, and the rest.
steps.stuck: Work the trade chain at the hut. Each trade hands back another piece of your kit; keep going until your inventory is whole.

steps.t: With your items back, head to the west side of the island. A big Tokay guardian statue stands there — but in the PAST its mouth is sealed. Find the nearby time portal.
steps.stuck: The dungeon door is the statue's MOUTH, and it only opens in the present. Look for the time portal near the statue on the island's west side.

steps.t: Play the Tune of Echoes at the portal to warp to the PRESENT. Now the guardian statue's mouth stands open — walk inside to enter the Moonlit Grotto.
steps.stuck: Warp forward with the harp, then approach the same statue. In the present its open mouth is the dungeon entrance.

steps.t: Cross-time note: in the PRESENT the statue's mouth is the Moonlit Grotto. The very same mouth in the PAST is a sealed path toward the Sea of No Return (an end-game area) — ignore it for now and enter in the present for this Essence.

sections.name: Inside the Grotto
sections.sub: The Seed Shooter and bouncing puzzles

steps.t: Work inward and fight through a Moldorm and the Arm Mimics — they mirror your movement, walking opposite to you. Clear the room and open the chest.
steps.stuck: Arm Mimics copy your steps in reverse. Maneuver so one backs into a corner or wall, then strike — or just slash as they pass.

steps.t: Claim the Seed Shooter. It fires Mystical Seeds as a projectile — and the seeds RICOCHET off walls, so you can curve a shot around a corner to hit a far switch or torch.

items.name: Seed Shooter
items.note: Fires Mystical Seeds that bounce off walls. Aim in 8 directions; load it with Ember, Scent, Pegasus, etc. The key tool of this dungeon.

steps.stuck: Equip the Seed Shooter, pick a seed type (Ember to light torches), aim a diagonal, and watch the seed rebound off the wall to reach what you can't hit straight-on.

steps.t: Use Ember Seeds with the Shooter to light the unlit torches — aim so the seed bounces off a wall to reach torches you can't face directly. Lit torches open the way forward.
steps.stuck: Stand back and fire a diagonal Ember Seed; let it bounce off the wall and into the torch. You don't have to be standing in front of it.

steps.t: Hit the crystal switches with the Seed Shooter to flip the spinning blocks and swing the bridge platforms into place, building a path across the room.
steps.stuck: Shoot the crystal switch (any seed works) to flip it. The bounce lets you trigger a switch tucked behind an obstacle.

steps.t: For the toughest switches, line up a bank shot: fire a seed at an angle so it glances off a wall and slips past the rotating obstacles to strike the switch beyond. Time it with the spin.
steps.stuck: Aim a diagonal so the seed ricochets off the wall and threads past the spinning blocks to the hidden switch — fire a beat before the gap lines up.

steps.t: Mini-boss: Subterror, a giant mole that burrows and surfaces to attack. When it pops up near you, dig it out with the Shovel, then hack it with your sword. It speeds up as the fight goes on.
steps.stuck: Watch the dirt mound move; the instant Subterror surfaces, use the Shovel to unearth it, then get a few sword hits in before it dives again.

steps.t: After the rotating-block and bridge puzzles, open the chest in the final approach for the Boss Key.

items.name: Boss Key
items.note: Opens the locked door to the Shadow Hag's chamber.

sections.name: Shadow Hag
sections.sub: Boss — bounce a seed off the wall

steps.t: Boss: Shadow Hag. She splits into four shadows that circle the room — touching any of them hurts you. Keep moving and don't get cornered while they rotate.
The four shadows merge into one small shadow with a face, and it spits out Moths. Kill the Moths — they drop hearts and seeds, so you can refill the Seed Shooter between rounds.
steps.stuck: Slash the Moths down; they're easy and they restock your seeds and health for the real attack.

steps.t: Here's the trick: you can't hit her by aiming at her. Turn so you're facing AWAY from the Shadow Hag, fire the Seed Shooter at the wall, and let the seed rebound back to strike her as she charges at you.
steps.stuck: Put your back roughly toward her, shoot a seed into the wall behind/beside you, and the bounce catches her on the rebound. Bait her charge, then fire just before she reaches you.

steps.t: Repeat the rebound shots through each Moth round. About six clean hits finish her — then grab the Heart Container she leaves behind.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Permanently adds one full heart to your life meter.

steps.t: With the Shadow Hag gone, step into the light and take the third Essence of Time — the Echoing Howl. Three down, five to go.

items.name: Echoing Howl
items.note: The 3rd Essence of Time. Restores more of the Maku Tree's power against Veran.

REGIONS.name: Skull Dungeon
REGIONS.tagline: A hook that swaps your place with the world.

steps.t: The fourth Essence sits in Skull Dungeon, beneath the volcano at Symmetry City in the northwest. In the PRESENT the city lies in ruins; in the PAST it thrives. You can't just walk in — the city's broken keystone, the Tuni Nut, has thrown everything off balance, and you must repair it across time first.
Go to PAST Symmetry City, where the town still stands, and get the broken halves of the Tuni Nut from the townsfolk. While it's broken, the city is off-balance and the dungeon stays sealed.
steps.stuck: The broken Tuni Nut comes from the past version of Symmetry City — talk to the residents there to receive it. (It's tied to a short errand among the past townsfolk.)

items.name: Tuni Nut
items.note: Broken — Symmetry City's keystone; must be repaired

steps.t: Take the broken Tuni Nut to Patch, the repairman, to have it mended. Patch is at the top of Restoration Wall — reaching him triggers a repair minigame: knock the Hardhat Beetles into the holes before the runaway mine cart smashes the Nut.
steps.stuck: Climb Restoration Wall to find Patch. In his minigame, push every Hardhat Beetle into a hole; stand on the floor switch to flip the track and send the cart around again so it doesn't hit the Tuni Nut before you finish. Clear it and he hands back the repaired Nut.

steps.t: Carry the repaired Tuni Nut back to PRESENT Symmetry City and set it on the pedestal in the central building. Restoring it rebalances the city and opens the path to the dungeon.
steps.stuck: Place the fixed Nut on the altar/pedestal inside the central building of present-day Symmetry City. The city reacts and the dungeon route opens.

steps.t: With the Tuni Nut restored, head to northern Symmetry City in the PRESENT. The erupting volcano is now a cascading waterfall, and a gap in the falls reveals the mouth of Skull Dungeon.
steps.stuck: Go to the waterfall at the top of present-day Symmetry City. The falls part to reveal a dark opening; walk into it.

steps.t: Keep the Harp ready. Skull Dungeon leans hard on flipping eras to reach blocked rooms — when a path dead-ends, check whether the same room differs in the other time. Vasu's rings and your Seed Shooter help between here and the entrance.

Work through the early rooms. Push the marked blocks to reach the Compass chest, and ride the mine-cart track to the chest that holds the Dungeon Map. Lava pools block many paths for now — you'll get the tool to cross them.
steps.stuck: The Map comes from riding the mine-cart line to its end chest; the Compass is behind a pushable block in an early room. Light torches and hit eye-switches to open barred doors.

steps.t: Collect Small Keys from the dungeon's puzzle rooms to open the locked doors barring your way deeper in.

items.name: Small Key
items.note: Several are hidden in Skull Dungeon's puzzle rooms

steps.t: Claim the dungeon's prize: the Switch Hook. Fire it at a marked object — a diamond block, a pot, a pillar — and you instantly SWAP places with it. That's how you cross the lava: hook something on the far side and trade spots with it. It also works on some enemies.
steps.stuck: Equip the Switch Hook to a button. Aim it at the diamond-shaped blocks or pots across a gap; when it grabs, you and the object switch places. It also stuns enemies it hits.

items.name: Switch Hook
items.note: Swaps Link's position with an object or stunned enemy; crosses lava and gaps

steps.t: Use the Switch Hook to leapfrog across the lava pools, swapping with diamond blocks and pots to reach the rooms that were cut off before. Flip eras with the Harp wherever a wall or floor differs across time.
steps.stuck: No block in reach? Look in the other era — the past/present version of the room often has a diamond block right where you need one to switch to.

steps.t: Reach the upper section and chain Switch Hook swaps with pots and platforms to grab the Boss Key, then make your way to the locked boss door.

items.name: Boss Key
items.note: Opens Skull Dungeon's boss door

steps.t: The boss is Eyesoar — a big floating eyeball ringed by four smaller eyes that orbit and dart at you. Hacking blindly at the cluster won't do it; the kill is the central eye, and the Switch Hook is the key.
steps.stuck: Ignore the small eyes for the kill — the damage all comes from the big central eye. Line up the Switch Hook on it.

steps.t: Fire the Switch Hook straight at the big central eye. You trade places with Eyesoar, leaving it stunned and momentarily helpless.
steps.stuck: Aim the Switch Hook directly at the central eyeball, not the small ones. The swap stuns Eyesoar — that's your opening.

steps.t: While Eyesoar is stunned from the swap, rush in and slash the central eye with your sword. Back off before it recovers, then Switch Hook the center again. Repeat until it falls.
steps.stuck: You only get a brief window each swap. Get one or two sword hits in, retreat, and re-hook the central eye. You can knock the small eyes away with the Hook or sword to buy room, but the kill comes from hitting the big eye while it's stunned.

steps.t: With Eyesoar destroyed, take the Heart Container it leaves behind — one more permanent heart on your life bar.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Permanently extends your life by one heart

steps.t: Step into the light and claim the fourth Essence of Time, the Burning Flame. Four down — return its glow toward the Maku Tree and press on against Veran.

items.name: Burning Flame
items.note: 4th Essence of Time — from Skull Dungeon

REGIONS.name: Crown Dungeon
REGIONS.tagline: A cane that builds blocks from nothing.

sections.name: Earn the Crown Key
sections.sub: Rolling Ridge — a Bomb Flower carried into the past

steps.t: Crown Dungeon is built into the cliff on Rolling Ridge in the present, and its door is locked. To open it you need the Crown Key — and to get that, you have to do a favor that crosses eras. Work through this section before heading to the door.

In the PRESENT, the Great Moblin has built a keep in Rolling Ridge and hoarded every Bomb Flower to put the Gorons out of business. Storm the keep and beat him to free the Gorons (Pegasus Seeds let you dash in fast).
steps.stuck: Stay in the PRESENT for this. Equip Pegasus Seeds and dash to close distance and reach the stairs up to the Great Moblin; whittle him down in his keep until the Gorons are freed.

steps.t: With the Great Moblin gone, the Gorons say they can grow Bomb Flowers again and hand you one. Keep it equipped — you're about to take it somewhere it doesn't normally exist.

items.name: Bomb Flower
items.note: A live, regrowing bomb plant — carry it through a time portal to the past.

steps.t: Carry that Bomb Flower into the PAST via a Rolling Ridge time portal. There the Goron Elder is pinned under a rockfall with no Bomb Flowers around — bring yours to the Gorons punching at the rock so they can blast it free.
steps.stuck: This is the cross-time trick: Bomb Flowers exist in the present but were lost in the past, so you must physically carry one back through a portal. Hand it to the Gorons working at the rubble trapping the Elder, and they'll use it to break the rock.

steps.t: The freed Goron Elder thanks you and gives the Crown Key. This unlocks Crown Dungeon.

items.name: Crown Key
items.note: Opens the locked door of Crown Dungeon on Rolling Ridge.

steps.t: Head to the Crown Dungeon door built into the cliff on Rolling Ridge and use the Crown Key to open it. Go inside.
steps.stuck: The entrance is set into the rock wall on Rolling Ridge. If the door is still sealed, you haven't freed the Elder yet — go back and finish the Bomb Flower errand.

sections.name: Into the Dungeon
sections.sub: Statues, switches, and the cane that makes blocks

steps.t: Work through the early rooms clearing enemies and hitting crystal switches to raise and lower the colored block barriers. Pick up the dungeon map and compass when you find them, and grab any Small Keys to pass locked doors.
Reach the statue room. Step on the switch to flash a pattern, then push the statues so they match that exact arrangement.
steps.stuck: Stand on the button to flash the pattern, then push each statue into the matching spot (try the red statue right, the blue one above it up, then the red one up and left). Match the pattern and a chest appears in the middle.

steps.t: Open the chest the statue puzzle unlocks to claim the Cane of Somaria — it conjures a solid block out of thin air that you can place and push. Blocks become your main tool for the rest of the dungeon.

items.name: Cane of Somaria
items.note: Creates one movable block (it stays until you make another or leave the room); use it to hold switches, weigh down platforms, and block paths.

steps.t: Get comfortable with the cane now: tap it to summon a block, tap again to remake it elsewhere, and push the block onto switches or across gaps. Many doors ahead open by parking a Somaria block on a floor switch.

Use Somaria blocks to solve the block-and-switch rooms on the way to the boss door — drop a block on a pressure plate to hold it, push blocks to reach ledges, and collect the Boss Key when you find it.
steps.stuck: If a switch keeps popping back up the instant you step off, summon a Somaria block and push it onto the switch to hold it down while you move on. In one dark room, push a block to feel out the invisible path to the center chest.

sections.name: Boss: Smog
sections.sub: A cloud you reassemble with blocks

steps.t: Smog is a puzzle boss, not a slugfest. It splits into two or three small clouds that slide along the walls and around any blocks — your sword can't catch them while they're scattered. The Cane of Somaria is the whole answer.
The little clouds glide along the edges and around blocks. Drop Somaria blocks in their paths to redirect them so they crash into each other — each collision merges clouds together.
steps.stuck: Place a Somaria block just ahead of a moving cloud to bend its path toward another cloud. Keep forcing collisions until all the small clouds have fused back into one full-size Smog.

steps.t: Once the clouds recombine into the whole Smog, it turns vulnerable. Rush in and hit it repeatedly with your sword while dodging the lightning bolts it spits at full size.
steps.stuck: Smog only takes sword damage in its reformed full-size state. Slash it fast, sidestep the lightning, and if it splits apart again just repeat the block-collision trick to rebuild it. It re-splits a few times before it dies.

steps.t: When Smog is destroyed, a Heart Container appears. Grab it to permanently raise your max hearts.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Adds one full heart to your maximum life.

steps.t: Step into the light to claim Sacred Soil, the fifth Essence of Time. Five down — return to the Maku Tree when you're ready to learn what to chase next.

items.name: Sacred Soil
items.note: The 5th Essence of Time. The soil that nurtures all life.

REGIONS.name: Mermaid's Cave
REGIONS.tagline: A cave split across the ages, and the sea.

steps.t: From Rolling Ridge you leave with two keys: the Mermaid Key (for the PAST cave) and the Old Mermaid Key (for the PRESENT cave). Mermaid's Cave is the same cave in both eras, so you'll carry both.
steps.stuck: If you don't have both keys yet, finish the Rolling Ridge errands first. Remember the pairing: the plain Mermaid Key opens the PAST entrance; the Old Mermaid Key opens the PRESENT one.

steps.t: Go to the cave on the eastern Rolling Ridge coast in the PAST. Swim into the waterfall and open the locked door with the Mermaid Key. You begin the dungeon in the PAST — and working through it here is what opens the cave up in the PRESENT.
steps.stuck: The entrance is behind a waterfall — swim straight into it. Wrong era? Stand on a time portal and play the Tune of Currents to drop into the past, then come back to the cave.

steps.t: This is the trickiest dungeon so far: you clear it across BOTH eras, hopping between them. Clear the PAST cave first to make the PRESENT cave reachable, then cross back. Whenever you hit a dead end, leave, flip eras at a portal, and try the same cave in the other time — the room layouts differ.

sections.name: The Mermaid Suit
sections.sub: A bat, a key, and a swimsuit

steps.t: Once you've opened the way through the PAST cave, travel to a time portal, play the Tune of Currents to reach the PRESENT, and enter the cave there with the Old Mermaid Key. The mini-boss, the Boss Key, and the Mermaid Suit are all on this PRESENT side.
steps.stuck: If the present cave looks blocked, you haven't finished enough of the past side yet — go back, clear further in the PAST, then return to the present.

steps.t: Work through the PRESENT cave until you reach the mini-boss Vire, a winged demon. Strike it until it splits into two bats, then wait for each bat to swoop at you and hit it as it charges. Clearing the room opens the way to the Boss Key.
steps.stuck: Don't chase the bats — stand still and let them dive at you, then swing. Two clean hits after the split finishes it.

items.name: Mermaid's Cave Boss Key
items.note: Found on the PRESENT side near Vire; opens the boss door.

steps.t: Push on to the Mermaid Suit chest, still in the PRESENT. Clear the block in front of it, then use the Switch Hook on the stone diamond to swap places with it and cross the gap to the chest.
steps.stuck: Can't reach the chest? Get the block out of the way first, then aim the Switch Hook at the stone diamond — you'll trade spots with it and land beside the chest.

steps.t: Open the chest to claim the Mermaid Suit — the dungeon's prize. Wear it and you can swim freely AND dive underwater, which you'll need for the flooded rooms in the PAST and for the boss.

items.name: Mermaid Suit
items.note: Lets Link swim in deep water and dive underwater; press the dive button to go down, again to surface.

steps.t: With the Mermaid Suit, return to the PAST cave. Dive through the flooded rooms to reach the boss door, open it with the Mermaid's Cave Boss Key, and drop into the watery arena — Octogon is fought here in the PAST.
steps.stuck: Can't cross a deep stretch of water? Equip the Mermaid Suit and hold the dive button to go under the surface.

steps.t: On the surface Octogon circles the platform with its armored back toward you. Wait for it to turn and SPIT a rock — that moment exposes its FACE (the red side). Slash the face with your sword, or ricochet a Seed Shooter seed off a wall to hit it while it's turned away.
steps.stuck: The armored side takes no damage — only the red FACE is vulnerable, and it shows mainly when Octogon turns to spit. If you can't line up a straight shot, bounce a seed off the wall with the Seed Shooter.

steps.t: When Octogon dives below the surface, DIVE after it with the Mermaid Suit. Underwater it always faces you, so its red face is constantly exposed — just slash it repeatedly with your sword. Alternate as needed: hit the face up top when it spits, then chase it under and sword it.
steps.stuck: Underwater is the easy phase — its weak face is always toward you. Equip the sword and keep slashing the red side; the paler armored side does nothing.

steps.t: Octogon bursts. Step into the light it leaves behind to take the Heart Container — your max hearts go up by one.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Permanently adds one heart to your life meter.

steps.t: Claim the sixth Essence of Time — Lonely Peak — floating where the boss fell. Six of the eight essences are now yours; return to the Maku Tree when you're ready to feed it the next essence.

items.name: Lonely Peak
items.note: The sixth Essence of Time. Strengthens the Maku Seed against Veran.

REGIONS.name: Jabu-Jabu's Belly
REGIONS.tagline: Inside the great fish, a longer hook.

sections.name: Reaching Jabu-Jabu
sections.sub: Cure King Zora, cleanse the seas, win entry

steps.t: Travel to the Zora Seas in the northeast. In the PRESENT the water is polluted and the Zoras won't let you near Lord Jabu-Jabu, their guardian fish. You'll have to clean the seas before you can enter.
steps.stuck: The dungeon is reached and entered in the PRESENT. You still use the PAST to fix the things that unblock it, but the door to the belly is in the present-day seas.

steps.t: In the PAST, King Zora is sick. Bring him a Potion (buy one in town) to cure him. Grateful, he hands you the Library Key.
steps.stuck: Use a time portal to reach the PAST Zora Seas. King Zora lies ill in his throne room — give him a Potion and he gives you the Library Key in return.

steps.t: Use the Library Key on the Eyeglass Island Library and work through it (across both eras) to obtain Fairy Powder.
steps.stuck: The library puzzle spans past and present — read the Book of Seals to learn the trick, then claim the Fairy Powder. It's the cure for the cursed Fairy Queen.

steps.t: Find the cursed Fairy Queen and use the Fairy Powder on her. Restored, she thanks you by cleansing all the poison from the Zora Seas.
steps.stuck: The pollution isn't something you scrub yourself — the Fairy Queen clears the whole sea the moment you break her curse with the Fairy Powder.

steps.t: With the present-day seas clean, you're hailed as a hero in Zora Village and King Zora grants you permission to enter Lord Jabu-Jabu. Head to the great fish's mouth in the present.
steps.stuck: Once the water is clear, just talk to the Zoras again — entry to the belly opens up. No shrinking or special item needed to get in.

sections.name: Inside the Great Fish
sections.sub: Raise and lower the water, win the Long Hook

steps.t: Enter Jabu-Jabu's Belly (present only). The dungeon is built around changing the WATER LEVEL — switches raise and lower the flood so you swim some rooms and walk others.
steps.stuck: When a path looks blocked, the water is usually at the wrong height. Hunt down the water-level switch (often a crystal/lever) that raises or drains the floor you're on.

steps.t: Mid-dungeon you fight the mini-boss Angler Fish. Toss a Scent Seed to make it inflate and surface, then strike the glowing light on its head.
steps.stuck: Plain sword swings bounce off. Use a Scent Seed to lure/deflate it up, then hit the light on top of its head — repeat until it's done.

steps.t: Claim the dungeon item: the Long Hook, an upgraded Switch Hook with far longer reach. Hook a distant block, post, or enemy and you SWAP places with it across the gap.

items.name: Long Hook
items.note: Upgraded Switch Hook — longer reach; swaps Link's place with the target.

steps.t: Use the Long Hook to cross wide water and pits: aim at a fixed target (often a stone diamond) on the far side and fire to swap across. Its extra range opens the back half of the dungeon.
steps.stuck: If you can't reach a switch or chest, look for any solid post, block, or stone diamond on the far side and Long-Hook to it to swap over.

steps.t: Keep adjusting the water levels and Long-Hooking across rooms until you collect the Boss Key, then make for the boss door deep inside the fish.

items.name: Boss Key
items.note: Opens the door to Plasmarine's lair.

sections.name: Boss: Plasmarine
sections.sub: Turn its own orb against it

steps.t: The boss is Plasmarine, a giant jellyfish, fought in a fully underwater room. Your sword does nothing here — you beat it entirely with the Long Hook.
steps.stuck: Don't waste hearts swinging the sword; it can't hurt Plasmarine. The whole fight is about timing the Long Hook on it.

steps.t: Plasmarine shifts between BLUE and RED. The energy orb it fires is always the SAME color as its current form. Every time you Long-Hook it, you swap places AND flip its color.
Wait for Plasmarine to fire an orb, then Long-Hook it right after. You swap places and its color flips — so the orb it just shot (the old color) now flies into the new-colored boss and damages it.
steps.stuck: Let it shoot first, THEN hook. The swap drops Plasmarine into the path of its own orb and flips it to the matching color so the orb connects. If it electrifies instead of shooting, just dodge and wait for the next orb. Repeat until it's destroyed.

steps.t: With Plasmarine defeated, grab the Heart Container it leaves behind.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Permanently adds one full heart to your life gauge.

steps.t: Claim the seventh Essence of Time: the Rolling Sea. Seven down — only one essence remains before the Maku Seed can fully wake against Veran.

items.name: Rolling Sea
items.note: The 7th Essence of Time, won inside Jabu-Jabu's Belly.

REGIONS.name: Ancient Tomb
REGIONS.tagline: The last dungeon, and the eighth Essence.

steps.t: First get the key item. Sail into the Sea of Storms (south of Labrynna, just west of Crescent Island) and swim aboard the Piratians' ship. Give the Cap'n the Zora Scale and he hands over the Tokay Eyeball — the item that opens the path to the final dungeon.

items.name: Tokay Eyeball
items.note: Fits the empty eye socket of the Tokay Guardian Statue to open the way to the Ancient Tomb.

steps.stuck: Can't reach the pirate ship? You need the Zora Scale first (from finishing the Zora storyline / Jabu-Jabu's Belly), which lets you into the rough Sea of Storms. Swim out to the trapped ship and trade the Cap'n the scale for the Eyeball.

steps.t: Now switch to the PAST and go to the west end of Crescent Island. Find the big one-eyed Tokay Guardian Statue and place the Tokay Eyeball into its empty eye socket. The way opens, revealing the cave that leads onward.
steps.stuck: Use it in the PAST, on the statue with the empty eye socket. (Don't confuse it with the open-mouthed Tokay idol in the PRESENT — that one is the Moonlit Grotto, the third dungeon.) Flip to the past and try the socket statue.

steps.t: Go through the cave and out into the Sea of No Return. Work through that short maze of currents — three trial rooms, then the open sea — to reach the entrance of the Ancient Tomb.
steps.stuck: In the Sea of No Return, getting lost loops you back to the start. Take it slow, follow the open channels, and the tomb door sits at the far end.

steps.t: This is the last dungeon — stock up first. Carry Bombs, the Seed Shooter loaded with Scent Seeds, plenty of Potion, and your best damage-reducing ring (the Blue Ring halves damage if you have it). You will need almost every tool you own.
The Ancient Tomb is the biggest dungeon in the game — a small ground floor over ever-wider basements. You'll see giant stone sarcophagi blocking paths everywhere; the dungeon item is what moves them.
Solve the floor puzzle to claim this dungeon's tool: the Power Glove. With it, Link can lift the huge sarcophagi (and other heavy blocks) that have been walling off rooms.

items.name: Power Glove
items.note: Dungeon item — lets you pick up and throw the heavy sarcophagi blocking the tomb.

steps.stuck: Once you get the Power Glove, use it on the sarcophagi to open the rest of the floor. If the chest hasn't appeared yet, finish the room's switch/tile puzzle first.

steps.t: Use the new Power Glove to lift and toss the sarcophagi out of your way, opening the routes deeper into the pyramid. Use it to clear paths and weigh down switches toward the Boss Key.
steps.stuck: Face a sarcophagus and press the Power Glove button to hoist it, then throw it onto a pit, switch, or just aside to free the path.

steps.t: Push deeper, beating the room puzzles, until you reach the Boss Key. Grab it, then make your way to the sealed boss door.

items.name: Boss Key
items.note: Opens the door to Ramrock, the final dungeon boss.

steps.t: Ramrock is a stone golem that changes its arms across FOUR phases — and each phase needs a different weapon. This is a tool-swap fight, so know which item beats which form before you go in. (The bushes in the room's south corners regrow Bombs and Scent Seeds.)
Phase 1 (Fists): Ramrock fires its two fists at you and reels them back. Use your SWORD to knock a thrown fist back into Ramrock's body. About three good hits ends this phase.
steps.stuck: Stand where a thrown fist passes right by you, then slash it back into the core — a fist only hurts Ramrock if you deflect it into him before it returns to the wall.

steps.t: Phase 2 (Crushing hands): It detaches from the wall and chases you, then stops to slam its two hands together to crush you. Drop or throw a BOMB between the open hands so the blast catches it as it closes. About three bomb hits clears this form.
steps.stuck: Time the bomb so it goes off right as the hands clap shut over it — set it between them, step back, and let it explode.

steps.t: Phase 3 (Shielded front): Ramrock guards its face and fires lasers/orbs. Fire a SCENT SEED from the Seed Shooter at the wall behind it so the seed RICOCHETS off the wall and strikes its unguarded back. About four banked shots.
steps.stuck: This is the Seed Shooter's whole trick — bounce the seed off the back wall onto its rear. Aim past Ramrock at the far wall while it faces you so the ricochet lands behind it. Out of Scent Seeds? Cut the corner bushes to restock.

steps.t: Phase 4 (Ball-and-chains): Ramrock arms itself with two wrecking balls and fires one at you. Quickly grab the OTHER ball with the POWER GLOVE, drag it back to the wall as far as the chain allows, and release so it springs back and slams Ramrock. Three slams finishes it.
steps.stuck: Grab the ball-and-chain with the Power Glove, pull it to full extension toward the wall, then let go — the recoil whips it straight back into Ramrock's body.

steps.t: Ramrock crumbles. A Heart Container appears — grab it to raise your max hearts.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Boss drop — adds one full heart to your maximum.

steps.t: Step into the glowing crest to claim the eighth and final Essence of Time: the Falling Star. With all eight essences gathered, the way to wake the Maku Tree's seed and confront Veran is open.

items.name: Falling Star
items.note: The 8th Essence of Time — the last one. Its eternal light guides the way to the other seven.

steps.stuck: That's all eight essences. Return to the Maku Tree in Lynna — the gathered essences awaken the Maku Seed, your weapon against Veran.

REGIONS.name: Confronting Veran
REGIONS.tagline: Wake the Maku Seed, free Nayru, undo the shadow.

steps.t: Before anything else, make sure you hold all 8 Essences of Time. Open the menu and confirm the Essence ring is full — if one is missing, go finish that dungeon first.
Return to the Maku Tree in the PRESENT, in the northeast of Lynna City. Walk up to the tree and speak with it to present the Essences.
Watch the Essences gather to the tree. The eight powers combine and the Maku Tree produces the Huge Maku Seed — the one thing that can pierce Veran's magic.
steps.stuck: Nothing to solve here. The cutscene only plays once all 8 Essences are collected; if it doesn't start, an Essence is still missing.

steps.t: The Maku Seed is yours. The Maku Tree tells you where Veran hides: the Black Tower that Queen Ambi has raised in the PAST, southwest of Lynna Village. Travel to the past and head there to end this.

In the PAST, go to the Black Tower southwest of Lynna Village. Stand at the entrance — the Maku Seed rises and, with the eight Essences, quells the barrier and fuses the three doorways into one. Step through into the tower.
steps.stuck: If the doorways don't merge, you don't have the Maku Seed yet — present all 8 Essences to the present Maku Tree first.

steps.t: Climb the turret. First a staircase maze: weave through the stairs to find the one leading up. Then a room ringed by flames — follow the flame that appears behind you when you climb; it points to the correct staircase. Any wrong stair drops you into a monster room you must clear before trying again.
This is the final stretch — there's no shop or safe room past the entrance. Top up hearts now: equip your best ring, carry Potion if you have it, and bring Bombs and your seeds (you'll need both).
At the top you reach Queen Ambi's throne — but Veran has been possessing her all along. The fight begins with Veran still inside Ambi.

sections.name: The Battle with Veran
sections.sub: Out of Ambi, then her three forms

steps.t: Phase 1 — pull Veran out of Ambi. Dodge Ambi's fireballs and the spiders she summons, then fire a Mystery Seed to force Veran's shadow up above Ambi's head. The moment it surfaces, use the Switch Hook to yank Veran out — now she's vulnerable. Close in and slash her with your sword.
steps.stuck: The sword alone won't hurt her while she's in Ambi. Mystery Seed first to surface the shadow, THEN Switch Hook to pull her free, THEN slash. Repeat if she ducks back in.

steps.t: Phase 2 — fairy form. Freed from Ambi, Veran becomes a fairy-like demon and conjures Dark Link copies that mirror your movement. Lure them into a corner so they bunch up, then strike. Hit Veran herself whenever she flies near or pauses to spit her orbs.
steps.stuck: The Dark Links move opposite to you. Back into a corner to stack them in one spot, step toward the middle, and sweep them all with one swing — then focus Veran between her orb attacks.

steps.t: Phase 3 — her three true forms. Veran now cycles between a TURTLE, a SPIDER, and a BEE. Each needs a different approach (next steps), but all finish with the sword.
steps.stuck: She rotates through the three shapes — don't panic at the changes. Match the method to whatever form is on screen and keep chipping her health down.

steps.t: Turtle form: she leaps high and crashes down to crush you, leaving acid where she lands. Use Pegasus Seeds to dart clear of each slam; when she stops and pokes her head out of the shell, rush in and slash the exposed head.
Spider form: she spits sticky webs that trap and hurt you. Throw a Bomb in front of her to stun her, then close in and hit her with the sword while she's frozen.
steps.stuck: The sword bounces off the spider until she's stunned — a Bomb dropped in front of her is what opens her up. Then swing.

steps.t: Bee form: she flies around shooting orbs and stingers and drops smaller bees that chase you (kill those for hearts if you're hurting). Stay mobile with Pegasus Seeds and slash her with the sword whenever she dips close.
Land the last hit on her final form. Veran is destroyed and the shadow lifts from Labrynna's past and present.
steps.stuck: There are no items to grab here — it's purely surviving each form and landing the sword: surface-and-hook for Ambi, then face/bomb/dodge-and-slash through turtle, spider, and bee.

steps.t: With Veran gone, Nayru the Oracle of Ages is free at last. Time flows true again, Queen Ambi is herself once more, and Labrynna's history is healed. The Harp of Ages has done its work.
Sit back for the ending. Note the secret password the Maku Tree gives at the close — write it down if you plan a linked game, where this story connects to its companion adventure.

enemies.name: Octorok
enemies.tactic: A burrowing critter that pops up to spit rocks at you in a straight line, then retreats. Sidestep the rock and close in for a single sword slash, or drop it from range with a seed. Harmless once you respect the spit — just don't stand in its line of fire.

enemies.name: Moblin
enemies.tactic: Bulldog-faced grunts that charge and, in groups, hurl spears or arrows. Two or three sword hits each. Use the Spin Attack when a pack closes in, and break line-of-sight on the ranged ones behind cover or by slipping into the other era.

enemies.name: Zol / Gel
enemies.tactic: Green blobs that hop toward you; a Zol splits into two smaller, faster Gels when struck, so finish the Gels quickly before they multiply your problems. A Spin Attack or a single clean hit on each handles them. Annoying in numbers but never threatening.

enemies.name: Buzz Blob
enemies.tactic: An electrified plant-blob — touching it or hitting it with the sword shocks YOU. Either keep your distance and use seeds/ranged tools, or feed it a Mystery Seed, which transforms it into a Cukeman you can then strike safely. Never just walk into one.

enemies.name: Bari / Biri
enemies.tactic: Jellyfish that drift and periodically crackle with electricity — don't touch them while they glow. A large Bari splits into two small Biri when hit. Wait for the charge to fade, then slash, or hit them with a ranged seed. Common around the water dungeons.

enemies.name: Stalfos
enemies.tactic: Skeleton swordsmen that flit erratically and parry careless attacks. Bait a lunge, then counter, or simply blow them apart with a Bomb. A Power Ring makes short work of them. Don't trade blows toe-to-toe — strike on their recovery.

enemies.name: Beamos
enemies.tactic: A stone eye-statue that rotates and fires a laser the instant its eye lands on you. It's invulnerable to the sword — either time your movement to cross behind it while it looks away, or destroy it with a Bomb. Mostly a positioning hazard, not a brawler.

enemies.name: Like Like
enemies.tactic: A tube-like maw that, if it grabs you, can swallow and STEAL your shield or a ring — mash buttons to escape fast, then kill it to recover the item. Hit it several times quickly from the side; don't let it pin you against a wall. Deal with it before it eats your gear.

enemies.name: Spiked Beetle
enemies.tactic: An armored beetle that's spike-side-up and immune to your sword head-on. Block its charge with your SHIELD to flip it onto its back (the Switch Hook also works), then slash the soft underside. A neat little shield-bash teaching enemy.

enemies.name: Pols Voice
enemies.tactic: A big-eared rabbit-ghost that bounces around and shrugs off sword hits. Its weakness is sound and explosives — a Bomb, or playing your animal companion's flute, makes it explode instantly. Don't waste sword swings; pop it with a Bomb or the flute.

enemies.name: Gibdo
enemies.tactic: A shambling mummy that only harms you by touch but soaks up hits. Hit it with an Ember Seed (or fire) to burn off the wrappings — it then turns into a Stalfos you can finish. Keep moving and don't let several corner you.

enemies.name: Wallmaster
enemies.tactic: A disembodied hand that drops from the ceiling; if it grabs you it drags you back to the dungeon entrance, undoing your progress. Watch for its shadow on the floor and step away, then strike it as it lands. Annoying more than deadly — mind the shadow.

enemies.name: Cheep Cheep
enemies.tactic: A pudgy fish that swims fixed patterns in the side-scrolling underwater sections. Slash it as it passes, or simply weave around its predictable path. Low threat.

enemies.name: Ropa
enemies.tactic: Snake/plant enemies that lie dormant like rope coils, then lunge when you draw near. A quick slash kills them; just don't get surprised by one you mistook for scenery. Filler foes in overworld grass.

enemies.name: Pumpkin Head
enemies.tactic: Boss of Spirit's Grave (Dungeon 1) — a spectral body topped with a jack-o'-lantern.
enemies.drops: Heart Container, Eternal Spirit (1st Essence of Time)
enemies.battle: GEAR: just your sword and the Power Bracelet (found in this dungeon). HOW: Slash the floating body a few times — avoiding the three energy balls it occasionally spits — until the pumpkin drops to the ground. Equip the Power Bracelet, walk up and LIFT the pumpkin; a tiny ghost flies out, exposed and vulnerable. Now hurl the pumpkin at the ghost, or throw it away and rush the ghost with your sword before it can reform. The whole fight is a loop: body → lift pumpkin → hit the ghost → repeat. Keep the pumpkin tossed away from the ghost so it can't re-merge.

enemies.name: Head Thwomp
enemies.tactic: Boss of the Wing Dungeon (Dungeon 2) — a giant four-faced head fought in a side-view room.
enemies.drops: Heart Container, Ancient Wood (2nd Essence of Time)
enemies.battle: GEAR: Bombs and Roc's Feather. HOW: Head Thwomp is hurt ONLY by a Bomb dropped into the open hole on its TOP. Climb the side ladder, get above it, and toss a Bomb into the opening — the face it's showing when the Bomb lands inside is the face it then acts on. GREEN throws fireballs (retreat to the top-right of the ladder), BLUE shoots a widening ring of orbs (hug the bottom of the ladder against the wall), PURPLE slams down and rains boulders (tuck under one of the small platforms), and RED is the hit you want — it takes damage and drops a recovery heart. Land four red hits and it falls. Patience over the ladder is everything: read the face and dodge accordingly between throws.

enemies.name: Shadow Hag
enemies.tactic: Boss of Moonlit Grotto (Dungeon 3) — a witch who hides among shifting shadows.
enemies.drops: Heart Container, Echoing Howl (3rd Essence of Time)
enemies.battle: GEAR: the Seed Shooter (loaded with seeds) plus Roc's Feather to hop her fireballs. HOW: She splits into FOUR shadows that slide around — you can't hit the right one while facing her, and touching the real shadow hurts. Wait until the shadows merge back into the witch, who then spits fireballs. The trick: turn your BACK to her and fire the Seed Shooter at the opposite wall — the seed ricochets off the wall and strikes her from behind. Repeat the bounce-shot a few times (jumping her projectiles with the feather) and she's history. The whole fight teaches the Seed Shooter wall-bounce: never face her, always bank the shot.

enemies.name: Eyesoar
enemies.tactic: Boss of Skull Dungeon (Dungeon 4) — a floating eyeball ringed by smaller orbiting eyes.
enemies.drops: Heart Container, Burning Flame (4th Essence of Time)
enemies.battle: GEAR: the Switch Hook and your sword. HOW: First deal with the small eyeballs orbiting Eyesoar — strike them when they pause/stop circling to clear them out. With the little ones gone, fire the Switch Hook at Eyesoar's core: the hook swaps places with it and STUNS it. Immediately rush over and slash the stunned core as many times as you can before it recovers and re-summons its orbiters. Repeat the cycle — clear orbiters, Switch-Hook the core to stun, sword it — until it pops. Don't bother attacking while the eyes are spinning; clear them first.

enemies.name: Smog
enemies.tactic: Boss of Crown Dungeon (Dungeon 5) — a unique PUZZLE boss, a horned gas-cloud that divides into pieces.
enemies.drops: Heart Container, Sacred Soil (5th Essence of Time)
enemies.battle: GEAR: the Cane of Somaria (from this dungeon) and your sword. HOW: Each round, blocks appear and Smog splits into two or three pieces that ride around the blocks' edges like Sparks — immune to your weapons in this form. Use the Cane of Somaria to CONJURE an extra block in their path so the pieces are diverted into each other and forced to recombine into the whole Smog. Once it's whole, it slowly floats at you, spitting lightning you can't block — dodge that and quickly hack it with the sword. It re-splits and comes back for several rounds; each round, place a cane-block to force a collision, then sword the reformed Smog. Pure positioning — think of it as a sliding-block puzzle that shocks you.

enemies.name: Octogon
enemies.tactic: Boss of Mermaid's Cave (Dungeon 6) — a giant octopus fought across land and water.
enemies.drops: Heart Container, Bereft Peak (6th Essence of Time)
enemies.battle: GEAR: the Seed Shooter and your sword, plus the Mermaid Suit you earn in this dungeon. PHASE 1 (land): Stand on the central platform and equip the Seed Shooter. Octogon circles with its shell facing center and only occasionally turns to spit a rock — dodge the rock and fire seeds into its exposed face/back when it turns. PHASE 2 (water): After enough hits it half-submerges. Dive in with the Mermaid Suit, equip your sword, and slash it underwater while swimming clear (it chases and tries to trap you in a bubble). When it floats back up, return to the platform and resume the Seed Shooter routine. Alternate land-shooter and underwater-sword until it's beaten. Keep moving in both phases; never let it pin you.

enemies.name: Plasmarine
enemies.tactic: Boss of Jabu-Jabu's Belly (Dungeon 7) — an electric jellyfish in a fully underwater room.
enemies.drops: Heart Container, Rolling Sea (7th Essence of Time)
enemies.battle: GEAR: the Long Hook (the upgraded Switch Hook). Your sword is USELESS here — consider equipping a defense ring. HOW: Plasmarine darts to your position and either fires an electric ball or electrifies itself on the spot. It alternates between a RED form and a BLUE form, and the energy ball it shoots is always the same color as its current form. The kill is to hit it with the Long Hook while its ball is in flight: the hook both displaces Plasmarine AND flips its color, so it gets struck by its OWN attack. Example: it shoots a red ball — Long-Hook it so it turns blue and is yanked into the path of that red ball. As its health drops it fires faster, so watch its glow. It's a timing/color-matching duel, not a sword fight.

enemies.name: Ramrock
enemies.tactic: Boss of the Ancient Tomb (Dungeon 8, the final Essence dungeon) — a levitating stone golem with swappable arms, fought in FOUR phases.
enemies.drops: Heart Container, Falling Star (8th and final Essence of Time)
enemies.battle: GEAR: your sword, Bombs, the Seed Shooter, and the Power Glove (found in this dungeon). PHASE 1 — FISTS: He launches his two hands at you; strike a launched fist with your sword so it flies back into Ramrock's body. A few good hits and he switches arms. PHASE 2 — CRUSHING CLAWS: He chases trying to crush you between two claws. Lay or throw a Bomb directly between the claws so they slam it (and it explodes on him) — the only way to hurt this form. PHASE 3 — SHIELDS/ORBS: He guards his head and fires orbs/eye-lasers while sliding side to side. Use the Seed Shooter to bank a seed off the wall BEHIND him so it bounces onto the back of his head. PHASE 4 — BALL-AND-CHAIN ARMS: He swings two ball-and-chain arms. Grab a hanging arm with the Power Glove, pull it toward the wall, then release to fling it into him. Read which arms he equips and switch gear to match — each phase wants a different tool.

enemies.name: Veran, Sorceress of Shadows
enemies.tactic: The final boss — the Sorceress of Shadows who possessed Nayru and Queen Ambi to drain Labrynna's past. The climactic Black Tower battle runs through FIVE stages and several transformations.
enemies.drops: Victory — the Maku Tree is freed and Labrynna is restored
enemies.battle: Stock a bottled Fairy and potion first — this is a long fight. STAGE 1 — POSSESSED NAYRU: You can't hit Nayru directly. Dodge Veran's energy balls and fire a MYSTERY SEED at her to force Veran's shadow up out of Nayru's head, then use the Switch Hook to YANK the shadow out — now sword it. STAGE 2 — POSSESSED AMBI: Veran jumps into Queen Ambi; same plan (Mystery Seed → Switch Hook → sword), but she also summons spiders/adds, so clear them while you work. STAGE 3 — FAIRY FORM: She becomes a fairy-creature, summons Dark Links that mirror your movement, and flies around firing energy balls (including a radial spray and a slow orb that briefly turns you into a helpless infant — wait it out). Sword her whenever you get an opening; ignore the mirror-clones. STAGE 4 — THREE MONSTER FORMS: She cycles through TURTLE (jumps and slams to crush you — sword her exposed face when she lands), SPIDER (spits a damaging web — throw a Bomb in front to stun her, then sword), and BEE (flies, summons bee swarms, fires speed-slowing stingers — hit her with the Seed Shooter or sword). Keep a fairy in reserve, match each form's counter, and outlast her. Beat the final form and Veran is destroyed for good.

basics.title: Sword Slash & the Spin Attack
basics.body: Tap the sword button for a quick slash; that handles most common foes in one or two hits. Hold the button until Link flashes, then release to unleash a Spin Attack that strikes every direction at once — perfect when enemies surround you. Upgrade to the L-2 Noble Sword (and later the Master Sword) at the Maku Tree and from secrets, and your damage roughly doubles. When a foe is armored or facing you, look for its exposed back or a moment it drops its guard rather than mashing.

basics.title: Time Travel to Reposition & Avoid
basics.body: The Harp of Ages is your defining tool: at a glowing time portal (or with the right harp tune) you slip between the PAST and the PRESENT, and the same patch of Labrynna can be wildly different in each era. In a fight or a tight spot, that means you can duck into the other age to escape a swarm, then return behind your enemies. Many obstacles only exist in one era — a wall, a pit, a block — so changing the past literally reshapes the ground you fight on. Think of time travel as a dodge and a puzzle key, not just a story beat.

basics.title: The Wall-Bouncing Seed Shooter
basics.body: The Seed Shooter fires whatever mystic Seed you've loaded (Ember, Scent, Mystery, etc.) in a straight line that RICOCHETS off walls. Aim it at a wall so the seed caroms around a corner or comes back to strike a foe you can't face directly — this is the exact trick the Shadow Hag boss demands. Ember Seeds light things and burn enemies; Scent Seeds lure and damage; Mystery Seeds have random effects and are key against shadow/possessed foes (they're what forces Veran's shadow out). Keep your Seed Satchel stocked and remember each seed type does something different when it lands.

basics.title: The Switch Hook — Swap Places
basics.body: The Switch Hook fires a hook that TRADES places with whatever it hits — a block, a pot, or an enemy. Use it to yank yourself across a gap by hooking a far block, to pull a switch-tile under your feet, or to swap a dangerous enemy into a pit or hazard. Upgraded to the Long Hook it reaches farther, and it's the key to bosses like Eyesoar (hook the core to stun it) and the Plasmarine fight. It costs nothing to use, so reach for it constantly as both a traversal tool and a weapon.

basics.title: Equipping a Magic Ring
basics.body: Throughout Labrynna you'll find magic Rings; take them to Vasu's shop in Lynna City to appraise unidentified rings for free. Once appraised, store them in your Ring Box and EQUIP one at a time for a passive bonus — the Power Ring boosts attack, the Blue/Armor-style rings cut damage taken, others give niche effects like cheaper shops or extra defense. Before a hard fight, swap to a defense ring (great for the underwater Plasmarine bout where your sword is useless) or a Power Ring to end it faster. Bigger Ring Boxes let you carry more, but only the equipped ring is active.

basics.title: Animal Companion & the Bottled Fairy
basics.body: Partway through, you befriend one animal companion — Ricky the kangaroo, Dimitri the Dodongo, or Moosh the bear (which one depends on how you got the flute) — that you summon with its flute to cross terrain, attack, or carry you safely past hazards, a mobile escape hatch in open areas. Just as vital: keep a Fairy bottled. A captured fairy automatically revives you with several hearts the instant you'd otherwise die, turning a fatal hit into a second chance. Stock potions and fairies before any dungeon boss, and don't be shy about retreating to a fairy fountain to refill.

COMPENDIUM.name: Wooden Sword
COMPENDIUM.effect: Link's starting blade and your basic melee weapon — swing it, or hold the button to charge a Spin Attack and release for a wider hit that does double damage. It's the level-1 sword, the base of the Wooden → Noble → Master upgrade line.
COMPENDIUM.where: Given to you by Impa near the very start, right after Nayru (the Oracle of Ages) is possessed by Veran — in a hidden clearing reached by pushing the triangle-marked rock, before you head west to Lynna Village. (In a Linked Game you already have a sword, so Impa hands you a shield instead.)

COMPENDIUM.name: Noble Sword
COMPENDIUM.effect: The level-2 sword upgrade — hits noticeably harder than the Wooden Sword and fires a sword beam when you're at full health. The strongest sword you can get in a normal, unlinked game.
COMPENDIUM.where: The reward at the end of the long trading sequence: trade your way to the Broken Sword from the Old Zora (in a cave on the Coast of No Return), then take it to Patch atop Restoration Wall (just south of Symmetry Village, in the Past) and win his Restoration Ceremony — push all eight Hardhat Beetles into the corner holes. (In a Linked Game it can also come from the Clock Shop Secret.)

COMPENDIUM.name: Master Sword
COMPENDIUM.effect: The legendary level-3 blade — stronger than the Noble Sword and, like it, fires a sword beam at full health. The top upgrade of the standard sword line.
COMPENDIUM.where: Linked-Game only (Ages linked with Oracle of Seasons via a password). If you already have the Noble Sword (earned earlier via the Clock Shop Secret) when you bring the Broken Sword to Patch, his Restoration Ceremony produces the Master Sword instead.

COMPENDIUM.name: Biggoron's Sword
COMPENDIUM.effect: A huge sword that swings in a full 180-degree arc (versus the normal 90-degree slash) for the biggest damage in the game — the single most powerful sword, stronger even than the Master Sword. The catch: it's held in both hands, so it takes up BOTH item slots and you can carry no shield while it's equipped. A bonus weapon, not part of the normal progression.
COMPENDIUM.where: Linked-Game / password reward at the Goron Shooting Gallery on Rolling Ridge (Past): tell the Goron Elder the correct secret, then score 300 points in the shooting minigame while wielding the sword to keep it. Not available in a single, unlinked playthrough.

COMPENDIUM.name: Wooden Shield
COMPENDIUM.effect: Your basic level-1 shield — hold it up to block weak enemy attacks and deflect some projectiles. Won't stop stronger or fiery attacks.
COMPENDIUM.where: Bought from the Item Shop in Lynna City (Present) for 30 Rupees. This is the shield most players start protecting themselves with.

COMPENDIUM.name: Iron Shield
COMPENDIUM.effect: The level-2 shield upgrade — sturdier than the Wooden Shield, it blocks more and stronger attacks. A worthwhile defensive step up.
COMPENDIUM.where: On Crescent Island, after the Tokay steal your gear: the thief hidden in a cavern on the island's south edge (reached by swimming there with the Mermaid Suit) hands back an upgraded Iron Shield instead of your old Wooden one. If a Like Like later eats it, a replacement can be bought from any shop for 50 Rupees.

COMPENDIUM.name: Mirror Shield
COMPENDIUM.effect: The level-3 top-tier shield — the best protection in the game, blocking attacks the lesser shields can't.
COMPENDIUM.where: Linked-Game only (with Oracle of Seasons): the final upgrade of the shield line, after you've already gotten the Iron Shield. It can't be obtained in a standalone Ages playthrough (a replacement sells for 80 Rupees in shops once unlocked).

COMPENDIUM.name: Magic Rings
COMPENDIUM.effect: A collectible system of equippable rings, each granting a passive power while worn. You find unappraised rings as drops, dig spots, Gasha-tree harvests and chest rewards, then have Vasu appraise them (the first appraisal is free, 20 Rupees each after) to make them usable; store and equip them via the Ring Box, which holds more as you upgrade it. Many rings carry over between Ages and Seasons in a Linked Game.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found throughout Labrynna (enemy/boss drops, hidden digging spots, Gasha-tree harvests, minigames). Take them to Vasu's jewelry shop in Lynna City to identify and equip them; expand your Ring Box to hold more at once.

COMPENDIUM.name: Power Ring
COMPENDIUM.effect: A Magic Ring (comes in L-1/L-2/L-3 grades): raises your sword's attack power, but in exchange you take more damage every time you're hit — a glass-cannon trade-off (the L-3 grade is Linked-Game only).
COMPENDIUM.where: One of the rings you collect and have Vasu appraise. Equip it from the Ring Box when you want to hit harder and can afford to play more carefully.

COMPENDIUM.name: Armor Ring
COMPENDIUM.effect: A Magic Ring (also tiered L-1/L-2/L-3): cuts the damage you take when hit, at the cost of lowering your own sword damage — the defensive opposite of the Power Ring (the L-3 grade is Linked-Game only in Ages).
COMPENDIUM.where: Collected as an unappraised ring and identified by Vasu in Lynna City. Good for tough rooms where survival matters more than speed.

COMPENDIUM.name: Like Like Ring
COMPENDIUM.effect: A transformation Magic Ring that turns Link into a Like Like — while worn you CAN'T use any of your items, but as a side effect Like Likes won't steal your equipped shield. A novelty/protective ring rather than a combat one.
COMPENDIUM.where: One of the many rings you can find and have Vasu appraise; equip it if you specifically want to walk through Like Like-infested areas without losing your shield (just remember items are disabled while it's on).

COMPENDIUM.name: Harp of Ages
COMPENDIUM.effect: Your time-travel instrument. Playing its tunes opens Time Portals and lets you cross between the past and present of Labrynna. You can't progress without it.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in a secret room of Nayru's house, where an imprint of Nayru gives you the Harp and teaches the Tune of Echoes.

COMPENDIUM.name: Tune of Echoes
COMPENDIUM.effect: The first Harp song. It awakens the Time Portals scattered across Labrynna so you can warp between past and present at those spots.
COMPENDIUM.where: Learned together with the Harp itself, taught by Nayru's imprint in Nayru's house near the start.

COMPENDIUM.name: Tune of Currents
COMPENDIUM.effect: Played in the past, it warps you instantly to the present from anywhere, no portal needed (past-to-present only). Also unlocks access to the L-2 Ring Box.
COMPENDIUM.where: Given by Tokkey (a tune-and-time researcher) when you play the Tune of Echoes for him, after rescuing Nayru.

COMPENDIUM.name: Tune of Ages
COMPENDIUM.effect: The full time-travel song: warps you freely between past and present from anywhere, in either direction. The most powerful Harp tune.
COMPENDIUM.where: Taught by Nayru after you defeat Veran's Possessed Nayru in Ambi's Palace and speak with the Maku Tree.

COMPENDIUM.name: Seed Satchel
COMPENDIUM.effect: Holds your Mystical Seeds and lets you use them by hand. Upgraded versions raise how many of each seed you can carry.
COMPENDIUM.where: A gift from the Maku Tree in the past after you save it.

COMPENDIUM.name: Ember Seeds
COMPENDIUM.effect: Light torches and burn certain bushes or enemies. Often needed to solve fire-lit puzzles and open dark rooms.
COMPENDIUM.where: Grown from Ember Seed trees; the first Mystical Seed type you receive.

COMPENDIUM.name: Scent Seeds
COMPENDIUM.effect: Give off a strong scent that lures or damages enemies; thrown or shot, they also work as a ranged attack.
COMPENDIUM.where: Grown from a Scent Seed tree found as you explore Labrynna.

COMPENDIUM.name: Pegasus Seeds
COMPENDIUM.effect: Sprinkle on yourself for a temporary speed/dash boost, letting you cross gaps and outrun hazards.
COMPENDIUM.where: Grown from a Pegasus Seed tree in the overworld.

COMPENDIUM.name: Gale Seeds
COMPENDIUM.effect: Create a whirlwind. Used by hand they warp you to any Mystical Tree you've found; shot, they blow weaker enemies away.
COMPENDIUM.where: Grown from a Gale Seed tree found during your travels.

COMPENDIUM.name: Mystery Seeds
COMPENDIUM.effect: Have a random effect — they can confuse enemies, reveal hints from owl statues, or trigger odd reactions. Handy for puzzles.
COMPENDIUM.where: Grown from a Mystery Seed tree in Labrynna.

COMPENDIUM.name: Seed Shooter
COMPENDIUM.effect: Fires Seeds across the room, and the seeds ricochet off walls to reach distant switches and targets. Central to that dungeon's bouncing-seed puzzles.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in Moonlit Grotto, the third dungeon.

COMPENDIUM.name: Power Bracelet
COMPENDIUM.effect: Lets you grab and lift heavy objects — pots, blocks, and bushes — to clear paths, expose switches, and find hidden stairs.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in Spirit's Grave, the first dungeon (used to lift the pot guarding the Boss Key).

COMPENDIUM.name: Roc's Feather
COMPENDIUM.effect: Lets Link jump, hopping over gaps, spikes, and pits. Essential for platforming and dodging.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in Wing Dungeon, the second dungeon.

COMPENDIUM.name: Switch Hook
COMPENDIUM.effect: Fires a hook that swaps Link's position with whatever it hits — an enemy, a block, or a diamond stone — letting you cross gaps and rearrange puzzles.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in Skull Dungeon, the fourth dungeon.

COMPENDIUM.name: Long Hook
COMPENDIUM.effect: The upgraded Switch Hook (Level 2): longer reach and a faster firing rate, so you can swap with far-off targets.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in Jabu-Jabu's Belly, the seventh dungeon, upgrading the Switch Hook.

COMPENDIUM.name: Cane of Somaria
COMPENDIUM.effect: Conjures a block you can push, carry, or place on switches; the block can also be hit to create more blocks for puzzles.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in Crown Dungeon, the fifth dungeon.

COMPENDIUM.name: Bombs
COMPENDIUM.effect: Throw or set them to blow open cracked walls, expose secrets, and damage enemies and bosses. Refilled at shops or from grass/enemies.
COMPENDIUM.where: First given by Queen Ambi after you bring her Mystery Seeds; capacity upgradable via Bomb Bag finds.

COMPENDIUM.name: Shovel
COMPENDIUM.effect: Digs up the ground to uncover buried stairs, items, and Rupees, and to clear dirt piles blocking your way.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found at the base of Ambi's Tower in Lynna Village in the past.

COMPENDIUM.name: Mermaid Suit
COMPENDIUM.effect: Lets Link dive and swim freely in deep and dark water that Flippers alone can't handle. Needed to explore the sea areas.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in Mermaid's Cave, the sixth dungeon.

COMPENDIUM.name: Zora's Flippers
COMPENDIUM.effect: Let Link swim across water he could otherwise only wade into. Later superseded underwater by the Mermaid Suit.
COMPENDIUM.where: Obtained in Cheval's Tomb beneath his gravestone in Yoll Graveyard.

COMPENDIUM.name: Strange Flute
COMPENDIUM.effect: Summons one of three animal companions — Ricky (kangaroo), Dimitri (Dodongo), or Moosh (bear) — to ride for travel and combat. Which one you get depends on how you obtain the flute, and you can only get one.
COMPENDIUM.where: After Moonlit Grotto (3rd dungeon): Ricky's by scoring 250+ in the Lynna Shooting Gallery; Dimitri's by buying the flute (150 Rupees) at the Lynna City Shop; Moosh's by default if you take neither, found in Fairies' Woods.

COMPENDIUM.name: Ring Box
COMPENDIUM.effect: Holds the Magic Rings you have equipped at once. The L-1 box carries one ring; L-2 carries three.
COMPENDIUM.where: L-1 Ring Box given by Vasu at his Lynna City jewelry shop; L-2 box bought (300 Rupees) at the Lynna City Shop, reachable once you have the Tune of Currents.

COMPENDIUM.name: The Eternal Spirit
COMPENDIUM.effect: The 1st Essence of Time. Collecting all eight nourishes the Maku Tree and is your overall quest goal.
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear Spirit's Grave (Dungeon 1) and defeat its boss, Pumpkin Head.

COMPENDIUM.name: The Ancient Wood
COMPENDIUM.effect: The 2nd Essence of Time, one of the eight you must gather to restore the Maku Tree.
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear Wing Dungeon (Dungeon 2) and defeat its boss.

COMPENDIUM.name: The Echoing Howl
COMPENDIUM.effect: The 3rd Essence of Time, gathered for the Maku Tree.
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear Moonlit Grotto (Dungeon 3) and defeat its boss.

COMPENDIUM.name: The Burning Flame
COMPENDIUM.effect: The 4th Essence of Time, gathered for the Maku Tree.
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear Skull Dungeon (Dungeon 4) and defeat its boss.

COMPENDIUM.name: The Sacred Soil
COMPENDIUM.effect: The 5th Essence of Time, gathered for the Maku Tree.
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear Crown Dungeon (Dungeon 5) and defeat its boss.

COMPENDIUM.name: The Lonely Peak
COMPENDIUM.effect: The 6th Essence of Time, gathered for the Maku Tree.
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear Mermaid's Cave (Dungeon 6) and defeat its boss.

COMPENDIUM.name: The Rolling Sea
COMPENDIUM.effect: The 7th Essence of Time, gathered for the Maku Tree.
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear Jabu-Jabu's Belly (Dungeon 7) and defeat its boss.

COMPENDIUM.name: The Falling Star
COMPENDIUM.effect: The 8th and final Essence of Time. With all eight, the Maku Tree can grant the Maku Seed for the final battle.
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear Ancient Tomb (Dungeon 8) and defeat its boss.

COMPENDIUM.name: Small Key
COMPENDIUM.effect: Opens one locked door inside the dungeon where it's found. Each dungeon has its own keys, used up on locked doors.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in chests, under pots, or by clearing rooms within each dungeon.

COMPENDIUM.name: Boss Key
COMPENDIUM.effect: Unlocks the boss door of its dungeon. One per dungeon, needed before you can face that dungeon's boss.
COMPENDIUM.where: Hidden in a chest deep in each dungeon, usually behind a major puzzle.

COMPENDIUM.name: Maku Seed
COMPENDIUM.effect: The sacred seed the Maku Tree grants once all eight Essences are gathered. It dispels the dark magic guarding the final foe, opening the path to the end.
COMPENDIUM.where: Given by the Maku Tree after you collect all 8 Essences of Time.

COMPENDIUM.name: Magic Ring
COMPENDIUM.effect: The ring system: collectible rings that grant passive powers when equipped (more sword damage, defense, special abilities, and more). Found rings must be appraised by Vasu (20 Rupees) before they can be worn, and stored in your Ring Box.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in chests, dug up, dropped by enemies, or grown in Gasha Nuts across Labrynna; appraised at Vasu's jewelry shop in Lynna City.

COMPENDIUM.name: Power Ring
COMPENDIUM.effect: A notable Magic Ring. Boosts your sword's damage, but you also take more damage while wearing it — a glass-cannon trade-off.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found as a Magic Ring during exploration; appraised and equipped via Vasu.

COMPENDIUM.name: Energy Ring
COMPENDIUM.effect: A notable Magic Ring. Replaces your charged Spin Attack with a Sword Beam (regardless of current health), giving a ranged sword shot.
COMPENDIUM.where: Typically found inside a Gasha Nut grown from a Gasha Seed; appraised at Vasu's.

COMPENDIUM.name: Blue Ring
COMPENDIUM.effect: A notable Magic Ring. Halves the damage Link takes, but also halves his sword power — pure defense at a cost.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found as a Magic Ring during the quest; appraised and equipped via Vasu.

COMPENDIUM.name: Like-Like Ring
