COMPENDIUM.effect: A novelty Magic Ring. Transforms Link's sprite into a Like Like — purely cosmetic, with no gameplay benefit.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found as a Magic Ring; appraised at Vasu's jewelry shop.

COMPENDIUM.name: Gasha Seed
COMPENDIUM.effect: Plant it in soft soil and it grows into a Gasha Tree; harvest the Gasha Nut for rewards like Rupees, rings, hearts, or rare items.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in chests and given as rewards; planted in the special Gasha plots around Labrynna.

COMPENDIUM.name: Piece of Heart
COMPENDIUM.effect: Collect four Pieces of Heart to gain one full extra Heart Container, raising your maximum health.
COMPENDIUM.where: Hidden across Labrynna — in caves, minigame rewards, sidequests, and secret spots in both eras.

COMPENDIUM.name: Heart Container
COMPENDIUM.effect: Permanently adds one full heart to your life meter. Found whole rather than in pieces.
COMPENDIUM.where: Awarded for clearing dungeons (defeating bosses) and a few special moments.

COMPENDIUM.name: Rupees
COMPENDIUM.effect: Labrynna's money, used to buy items, appraise rings (20 each), and pay for shop goods and minigames. Carry capacity grows with bigger Wallets.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in grass, pots, chests, dug up, dropped by enemies, and won at minigames throughout both eras.

quests.name: The Long Trade: from a Poe Clock to the Noble Sword
quests.giver: Clock Poe (Yoll Graveyard, near Spirit's Grave)
quests.location: All across Labrynna, past and present (starts in Yoll Graveyard)
quests.reward: The Noble Sword (Level-2) — a stronger replacement for your Wooden Sword
quests.oneLine: Labrynna's 12-step swap chain: hand off one odd item after another until a ghost's broken sword becomes the Noble Sword.
quests.how: Each person wants the item the last one gave you, so trade strictly in order — you carry only one trade item at a time. The chain: (1) talk to the Clock Poe in Yoll Graveyard for the POE CLOCK; (2) give it to the Postman at the Lynna Village Post Office for STATIONERY; (3) give that to the 'Hand in the Toilet' in the house just below the post office for the STINK BAG; (4) give it to the Tokay cook on Crescent Island for the TASTY MEAT; (5) give that to the hungry Happy Mask Salesman at the Mask Shop near Lynna City for the DOGGIE MASK; (6) give it to Mamamu Yan (the dog breeder) in Lynna City for the DUMBBELL; (7) give it to Thomas in Symmetry Village (in the PAST) for the CHEESY MUSTACHE; (8) give that to the Comedian in Lynna City for the FUNNY JOKE; (9) tell the joke to the gloomy boy Dekadin in Lynna Village for the TOUCHING BOOK; (10) carry it until Maple flies by (after you defeat about 30 enemies) and crash into her to get the MAGIC OAR; (11) give the oar to Rafton on the South Shore (PAST) for the SEA UKULELE; (12) give it to the Old Zora in his cave for the BROKEN SWORD. Finally take the broken sword to Patch at Restoration Wall (south of Symmetry Village, in the PAST) and clear his block-pushing puzzle to forge the NOBLE SWORD. Fully optional — nothing here is needed to beat the game.

quests.name: Vasu's Ring Appraisals
quests.reward: Equippable Magic Rings (effects from extra power to fun cosmetic transformations)
quests.oneLine: Every Magic Ring you find shows up as an unidentified 'secret' until Vasu appraises it — then you can wear it.
quests.how: Magic Rings drop all over Labrynna but start out unidentified and unusable. Bring them to Vasu Jewelers in Lynna City and pick Appraise (20 Rupees each) to reveal what a ring does and unlock it for your Ring Box. You can only equip a limited number at once (your Ring Box capacity upgrades over the game), and duplicates can be sold back to Vasu for 30 Rupees. Vasu gives you your first ring and the Ring List early on, and the list lets you track which ring numbers you're still missing.

quests.name: The Magic Ring Collection (64 rings)
quests.location: Gasha Nuts, Maple, fortune-teller, chests, shops, minigames — everywhere
quests.reward: A full ring collection (and the satisfaction of completing it)
quests.oneLine: There are 64 collectible Magic Rings shared across the Oracle games; a few are genuinely useful, many are just for fun.
quests.how: Rings come from many sources: cracking open Gasha Nuts, bumping into Maple, Ring Fortunes from the fortune-teller, a handful in chests, and shop/minigame prizes. Useful early picks include the Power Ring (more melee damage), Blue Ring (take less damage), and Maple's Ring. Many rings are silly transformations (turn into a Like-Like, an Octorok, etc.) with no combat value — collect them for the set, not for power. The Ring List at Vasu shows exactly which numbers you're missing.

quests.name: Chasing Maple
quests.reward: Rings, Gasha Seeds, hearts, and Rupees
quests.oneLine: Maple zooms across the screen on her broom; crash into her and grab everything that spills.
quests.how: After you defeat about 30 enemies, Maple flies across certain overworld screens. Run into her broom to knock both of you down, then quickly pick up everything that scatters before she does — Rings, Gasha Seeds, hearts, and Rupees. Wearing Maple's Ring halves the kills needed for her to appear (about 15 instead of 30), so equip it if you're farming her. She's also step 10 of the trading sequence — she hands over the Magic Oar.

quests.name: Choosing Ricky, Dimitri, or Moosh
quests.location: Nuun Highlands (locks in after the 3rd dungeon, Moonlit Grotto)
quests.reward: A permanent animal partner with a unique traversal ability
quests.oneLine: One of three animal friends joins you for the rest of the game — and which one is decided by the flute you're carrying when you reach Nuun Highlands.
quests.how: Your partner is locked in when you reach the Nuun Highlands after clearing the third dungeon, Moonlit Grotto. The deciding factor is which Strange Flute you carry in: buy the flute from the Lynna City shop to get DIMITRI (a Dodongo who swims against currents and up waterfalls), score 250 at the Shooting Gallery to earn the flute that gives RICKY (a kangaroo who leaps cliffs and gaps and boxes enemies), or arrive with no flute at all to default to MOOSH (a bear who flies short distances). At Nuun Highlands your partner's ability helps you find the three lazy carpenters needed to extend the bridge. The choice is mostly flavor — each can reach a few optional spots. Whistle with the flute to summon your partner on the overworld.

quests.name: Feeding and riding your companion
quests.location: Overworld, anywhere your partner can travel
quests.reward: Access to optional ledges, gaps, and shortcuts; some hidden Heart Pieces and rings
quests.oneLine: Summon your animal friend with their flute to cross terrain Link can't on foot.
quests.how: Once your companion is chosen, use their flute on the overworld map to call them, then mount up. Use Ricky's jumps to clear pits and climb low cliffs, Dimitri's swimming to fight currents and ride waterfalls, or Moosh's hop-glide to float over gaps. Several optional Heart Pieces and ring spots are only reachable while riding, so revisit old areas with your partner after you have them.

quests.name: Pieces of Heart hunt
quests.location: All over Labrynna, past and present
quests.reward: Each 4 pieces = one new Heart Container (more life)
quests.oneLine: Scattered quarter-hearts that combine into full Heart Containers — several are tucked behind animal abilities or minigames.
quests.how: Pieces of Heart hide in chests, minigame prizes, and out-of-the-way corners across both eras; collect four to gain a permanent Heart Container. Some reward minigame skill (the Goron Dance, the Shooting Gallery) or require your animal companion to reach a ledge. Sweep Lynna City and the Maku Tree area as you pass through, and re-check regions after you gain new tools like the Seed Shooter, Switch Hook, and Mermaid Suit.

quests.name: Cross-time Heart Pieces and chests
quests.reward: Heart Pieces, rings, and Gasha Seeds reachable only by changing the past
quests.oneLine: The signature Ages puzzle: alter something in the PAST so a blocked spot opens up in the PRESENT — and a reward appears.
quests.how: With the Harp of Ages you warp at time portals between past and present, and changes you make long ago reshape the world today. Many optional rewards depend on this: plant or move something, drain or fill water, or knock down an obstacle in one era to open a path or reveal a chest in the other. When a Heart Piece or chest looks unreachable, find the matching spot in the other time period and ask what you could change there first. The Seed Shooter (which bounces seeds off walls) and Switch Hook also open previously-blocked optional chests on a return visit.

quests.name: Gasha Seeds & Gasha Trees
quests.location: 16 patches of Soft Soil across Labrynna
quests.reward: Gasha Nuts containing Rings, Rupees, hearts, and occasional rare items
quests.oneLine: Plant a teardrop-shaped Gasha Seed in soft soil, kill enemies to make it grow, then crack the nut for loot.
quests.how: Find a patch of Soft Soil and plant a Gasha Seed in it. The tree matures as you defeat enemies and roam the world, then bears a Gasha Nut you can slash open. Nut contents range from Rupees and hearts up to Magic Rings, and the quality scales with how active you've been — so wander and battle before harvesting. There are 16 soil patches but far more than 16 seeds available, so keep replanting; this is the best steady source of rings.

quests.name: Wild Tokay (meat-feeding game)
quests.reward: A story item (Scent Seedling) the first time, plus prizes on repeat play
quests.oneLine: A required Tokay feeding game: hand a piece of meat to every Tokay that runs past, miss none, and win.
quests.how: On Crescent Island the Tokay run you through a feeding game: Tokay dash past on the left and right, and you must give meat to every one as they appear. Skip even one and you fail and start over; feed them all and you win. The first clear ties into the main story, and the game can be replayed for extra prizes.

quests.name: Goron Dance & the Shooting Gallery
quests.location: Rolling Ridge (Goron Dance) and Lynna City (Shooting Gallery)
quests.reward: Heart Pieces, rings, and Rupees for high scores
quests.oneLine: Rhythm and aim challenges that pay out collectibles for doing well.
quests.how: In the Goron Dance, copy the instructor's left/right steps in rhythm — go too early or late and you miss; a clean run earns prizes including a Heart Piece. The Shooting Gallery rewards accuracy with Rupees and prizes, and scoring 250 there earns the Strange Flute that makes Ricky your animal companion. These are optional and replayable, so come back once you have steadier aim.

quests.name: Linked game: passwords with Oracle of Seasons
quests.location: Password screen at game start (uses a Secret from the other Oracle)
quests.reward: Bonus items, extra story, rings, and a Heart Container carried between games
quests.oneLine: Beat Oracle of Seasons first, type its Secret into Ages, and play a 'Linked Game' with extra content.
quests.how: Finishing Oracle of Seasons gives you a Secret password at the credits. Start a new Ages file, choose to enter a Secret, and type it to begin a LINKED GAME instead of a normal one. Throughout the linked game, NPCs hand you more passwords to carry back to Seasons (and vice versa), unlocking bonus rings, items, and continued story — the two games' plots fully connect only when linked. Keep a pen handy; the codes are long strings of symbols entered exactly.

quests.name: The true final battle: Twinrova & Ganon
quests.location: Room of Rites (only in a Linked Game)
quests.reward: The true ending of the Oracle saga + the 'Hero's Secret' password
quests.oneLine: Only a linked playthrough opens the real final fight, where the witches Twinrova try to resurrect Ganon.
quests.how: In a normal (unlinked) game you beat Veran and the story ends there. In a LINKED game, after Veran the Room of Rites opens: you face the twin sorceresses Koume and Kotake, who merge into Twinrova, and then Ganon himself as the true final boss. Ganon is tough — to damage him you need spin attacks with the Noble Sword, or normal hits with the Master Sword (the Biggoron's Sword won't hurt him). Win and you receive the 'Hero's Secret' password (which can start a fresh Ages game with 4 hearts and the Victory Ring) and the true ending of the Oracle saga.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Maku Tree (Great Fairy cave + seed restock)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Northeast Lynna City (present). The Maku Tree grows as you gather Essences of Time: once it starts growing (after the 1st Essence, Eternal Spirit) it opens a cave holding a Great Fairy, alongside the present-day Ember/seed bushes. It's your central hub all game.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: The Great Fairy fully heals Link, and the seed bushes in the Maku Tree restock his seeds — the most convenient heal/refill in the game

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Nuun Highlands Fairy Fountain
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Present era. Enter the cave on the north edge of Nuun Highlands (the same screen also has a high-level Gasha planting spot).
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Free full heal — refills all your hearts

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Talus Peaks Fairy Pool
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Present era, southwestern corner. In the PAST, set the water flowing eastward and push the vine bulb to the western side; warp back to the present with the Harp's Tune of Currents (or Tune of Ages), then climb the new vines up to the pool.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Free full heal — refills all your hearts

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Crescent Island Fairy Fountain
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Present era. From the screen where the crabs scuttle about, head east to reach the fairy fountain.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Free full heal — refills all your hearts

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Vasu Jewelers (Vasu)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: The jewelry shop in Lynna City (present). Visit early in the game, then return anytime to have Vasu appraise the Seed Rings you find.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Grants the L-1 Ring Box (holds 1 ring) free on your first visit, then appraises Seed Rings into usable Magical Rings (first appraisal free; 20 Rupees each thereafter)

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Lynna City Shop (hidden 2nd floor)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: A hidden upper room of the Lynna City shop, reached by using the Harp's Tune of Ages to time-warp from the past up into the present-day second floor.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Sells the L-2 Ring Box (holds 3 rings) for 300 Rupees — a permanent ring-storage upgrade

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Crescent Island Tokay (shield thief)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Present era. The Tokay steal all your gear when you land; recover items one by one by talking to the Tokay around the island. The one in the cavern on the island's south edge can only be reached by swimming there with the Mermaid Suit.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Returns your Wooden Shield upgraded to the Iron Shield (a one-time shield upgrade — the best shield in a non-linked game)

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Talus Peaks Bomb-Pond Fairy
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Present era, by the warning sign at the pond in Talus Peaks. Throw a Bomb into the water and a red Fairy rises, materializes a Golden Bomb and a Silver Bomb, and asks which you dropped. Answer honestly — you dropped neither, just a regular Bomb.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Rewards your honesty by raising your Bomb capacity by 20 (carry more Bombs). Claiming the Silver (or Gold) Bomb and confirming the lie makes her take all your Bombs.

RUNES.name: Harp of Ages
RUNES.from: From an altar in the hidden basement of Nayru's house in Lynna City — push the statue to reveal the stairs down, near the start of the game.
RUNES.what: Your signature tool. Play it at glowing Time Portals to travel between the PRESENT and the distant PAST. Because the two eras are the same land 400 years apart, a change you make in the past reshapes the present — the core of nearly every puzzle.
RUNES.tip: Three different tunes do three different things. You start with only the Tune of Echoes; the other two come later. Equip the Harp to a button to play whichever tune you've selected.

RUNES.name: Tune of Echoes
RUNES.from: You already know it when you pick up the Harp of Ages in Nayru's basement — it's the Harp's first tune.
RUNES.what: The first Harp tune. It awakens (activates) the many dormant Time Portals scattered across Labrynna so you can use them to jump eras.
RUNES.tip: If a time portal looks faded/inactive, stand on it and play this tune to wake it up.

RUNES.name: Tune of Currents
RUNES.from: Taught by Tokkey, the tune researcher, in a hidden cave at Talus Peaks in the PAST — play the Tune of Echoes for him and he teaches it.
RUNES.what: The second Harp tune. Play it anywhere to warp instantly from the PAST back to the exact matching spot in the PRESENT — no portal needed.
RUNES.tip: Huge time-saver and required for several puzzles where you must drop something in the past and reappear at that spot in the present.

RUNES.name: Tune of Ages
RUNES.from: Taught by Nayru late in the game (after you rescue her from Veran and speak with the Maku Tree).
RUNES.what: The third and final Harp tune. It unlocks the Harp's full power: travel freely between past and present from anywhere, in either direction.
RUNES.tip: Once you have this, you no longer need to hunt for portals — you can swap eras on the spot.

RUNES.name: Wooden Sword
RUNES.from: Given by Impa near the start — she entrusts it to you on Zelda's behalf as you set out to find Nayru.
RUNES.what: Your starting blade and main weapon. Tap to slash; hold to charge a Spin Attack that hits all around you.
RUNES.tip: Hold the button until the sword flashes and Link shakes, then release for the Spin Attack — great for crowds.

RUNES.name: Noble Sword
RUNES.from: Finish the trading sequence to receive the Broken Sword from the Old Zora on the Sea of No Return (in the PAST), then take it to Patch atop Restoration Wall and beat his minigame — he repairs it into the Noble Sword.
RUNES.what: The second sword. Double the Wooden Sword's power, and it can fire a sword beam when your hearts are full.
RUNES.tip: Keep your hearts topped off to shoot the beam — free ranged damage on every full-health swing.

RUNES.name: Master Sword
RUNES.from: Linked Game only (Ages linked to a completed Oracle of Seasons, via password): King Zora upgrades your Noble Sword into it. Not obtainable in a standalone Ages playthrough.
RUNES.what: The final, most powerful blade, with a long-range sword beam at full hearts.
RUNES.tip: This is a reward for playing Ages linked to a Seasons file; a first-time solo run tops out at the Noble Sword.

RUNES.name: Biggoron's Sword
RUNES.from: Linked Game only: score 300+ at the Goron Shooting Gallery in the PAST and the Goron Elder hands it over (a secret unlocked by linking with Oracle of Seasons).
RUNES.what: A massive two-handed sword that hits very hard, but takes up two item slots and blocks your shield while held.
RUNES.tip: Linked-game only. It trades your shield for raw power — swap back to a one-handed sword when you need to block.

RUNES.name: Wooden Shield
RUNES.from: Bought from the Lynna City shop (or given early on).
RUNES.what: Your basic shield. Hold it up to block enemy shots and stop weak charging foes.
RUNES.tip: A shield only blocks what you're facing — keep it pointed at the threat.

RUNES.name: Iron Shield
RUNES.from: Shop upgrade — buy the Level-2 shield as the game progresses.
RUNES.what: A sturdier shield that blocks attacks the Wooden Shield can't.
RUNES.tip: Some enemies will eat or break a weaker shield — upgrading keeps you covered.

RUNES.name: Mirror Shield
RUNES.from: The strongest (Level-3) shield — a top-tier shop upgrade.
RUNES.what: The best shield — blocks the most, and its mirrored face can reflect certain magic and beam attacks back at enemies.
RUNES.tip: Useful against beam-throwing enemies; face them and let the shield bounce the shot home.

RUNES.name: Seed Satchel
RUNES.from: Given by the Maku Tree, already filled with your first Ember Seeds; capacity upgrades are found later.
RUNES.what: Holds your magic Seeds and lets you scatter them or use them with seed-powered items. Upgrades raise how many of each seed you can carry.
RUNES.tip: It refills at the seed trees dotted around Labrynna — walk into a tree to top up the matching seed for free.

RUNES.name: Ember Seeds
RUNES.from: Ember seed trees; you get your first batch with the Seed Satchel from the Maku Tree.
RUNES.what: Fire seeds. Light torches and dark rooms, burn certain bushes/webs, and damage or stun enemies.
RUNES.tip: The all-purpose 'light it / burn it' seed — lighting paired torches opens many doors.

RUNES.name: Scent Seeds
RUNES.from: Scent seed trees, found as you progress through Labrynna.
RUNES.what: Smelly seeds that attract enemies and also deal solid damage when thrown or shot.
RUNES.tip: Loaded into the Seed Shooter they're a strong attack; on the ground they lure enemies to a spot.

RUNES.name: Pegasus Seeds
RUNES.from: Pegasus seed trees.
RUNES.what: Speed seeds. Use one to dash at high speed for a short time (and they briefly stun enemies you hit).
RUNES.tip: Combine with Roc's Feather for longer running jumps across wide gaps.

RUNES.name: Gale Seeds
RUNES.from: Gale seed trees.
RUNES.what: Whirlwind seeds. Drop one to warp instantly to any seed tree you've visited; thrown, they blow weaker enemies clean off the screen.
RUNES.tip: Your fast-travel system — use them to zip back to a tree near a dungeon instead of walking.

RUNES.name: Mystery Seeds
RUNES.from: Mystery seed trees.
RUNES.what: Random-effect seeds — they may trigger any of the other seeds' effects. They also activate certain owl statues and puzzle triggers.
RUNES.tip: Needed for some statue/puzzle hints; in combat the effect is a gamble, so save them for puzzles.

RUNES.name: Seed Shooter
RUNES.from: Found in the 3rd dungeon, the Moonlit Grotto.
RUNES.what: Fires whichever seed you have loaded — and the shot can travel at angles and ricochet off walls, letting you hit switches and enemies around corners.
RUNES.tip: Aim it diagonally and bank shots off walls to flip far-off switches the puzzle expects you to reach.

RUNES.name: Power Bracelet
RUNES.from: Found in the 1st dungeon, Spirit's Grave.
RUNES.what: Lets you lift and carry heavy pots, bushes, and certain blocks, then throw them.
RUNES.tip: Stand against an object and hold the button to grab; release to throw it at enemies or onto switches.

RUNES.name: Power Glove
RUNES.from: An upgrade found in the 8th and final dungeon, the Ancient Tomb.
RUNES.what: Upgrades the Power Bracelet so you can lift even the heaviest objects, like the large black rocks that block paths.
RUNES.tip: Backtrack to those immovable black boulders after the Ancient Tomb — you can finally move them now.

RUNES.name: Roc's Feather
RUNES.from: Found in the 2nd dungeon, the Wing Dungeon.
RUNES.what: Lets Link jump — over gaps, pits, spikes, and low obstacles.
RUNES.tip: Tap to hop a tile; jump while dashing with Pegasus Seeds to clear wider gaps.

RUNES.name: Switch Hook
RUNES.from: Found in the 4th dungeon, the Skull Dungeon.
RUNES.what: A hook that, instead of pulling things to you, SWAPS Link's position with whatever it grabs — an enemy, a block, or a pot — letting you cross gaps and rearrange puzzles.
RUNES.tip: Hook a block across a pit to instantly teleport to its side; you can also swap with hookable enemies to reposition mid-fight.

RUNES.name: Long Hook
RUNES.from: An upgrade found in the 7th dungeon, Jabu-Jabu's Belly.
RUNES.what: A longer, faster Switch Hook — same position-swap, but it reaches much farther.
RUNES.tip: It replaces the Switch Hook; once you have it, those just-too-far swaps become possible.

RUNES.name: Cane of Somaria
RUNES.from: Found in the 5th dungeon, the Crown Dungeon (and used to beat its boss, Smog).
RUNES.what: Creates a block you can carry, ride, or place on switches; the block can be destroyed with a sword slash.
RUNES.tip: Drop a block on a floor switch to hold it down, then walk free — a classic 'weigh down two switches at once' solver.

RUNES.name: Bombs
RUNES.from: First obtained early in your adventure; a Bomb Bag upgrade raises capacity later.
RUNES.what: Throwable explosives that blow open cracked walls, destroy obstacles, and damage enemies.
RUNES.tip: Cracked or oddly-plain walls often hide rooms — set a bomb next to them. Restock at shops or by cutting grass.

RUNES.name: Shovel
RUNES.from: From a construction worker at the base of Ambi's Tower in Lynna Village, in the PAST.
RUNES.what: Digs up soft ground to uncover buried stairs, items, and hearts, and clears small mounds of dirt blocking paths.
RUNES.tip: Dig spots that look disturbed or dead-end you — buried staircases and rupees are everywhere.

RUNES.name: Mermaid Suit
RUNES.from: Found in the 6th dungeon, Mermaid's Cave.
RUNES.what: An upgrade to the Flippers: lets Link swim in deep water AND dive underwater to explore submerged areas — needed to reach the underwater Zora area and Jabu-Jabu's Belly.
RUNES.tip: Press the button while swimming to dive; you can swim freely against currents that used to push you back.

RUNES.name: Zora's Flippers
RUNES.from: Found in Cheval's Tomb in the Yoll Graveyard, before the Mermaid Suit.
RUNES.what: Let Link swim across deep water that he'd otherwise drown in.
RUNES.tip: This is the first 'water-traversal' item; the Mermaid Suit later upgrades it, adding diving.

RUNES.name: Flute
RUNES.from: Your Strange Flute becomes one specific animal's flute based on how you get it: buy it from the Lynna City shop → Dimitri; score 50+ at the Shooting Gallery → Ricky; get no flute and visit Nuun Highlands → Moosh.
RUNES.what: Calls your one animal companion to ride. You only ever get one of the three in a playthrough.
RUNES.tip: Play it in the overworld to summon your partner; you can't switch partners later, so the choice is permanent for that file.

RUNES.name: Ricky
RUNES.from: Your companion if you earned the Strange Flute by scoring 50+ in the Shooting Gallery minigame.
RUNES.what: A boxing kangaroo. Ride him to leap up cliffs and over holes, and punch enemies with his gloves.
RUNES.tip: Ricky's high jumps reach ledges you can't on foot — handy for cliff shortcuts.

RUNES.name: Dimitri
RUNES.from: Your companion if you bought the Strange Flute from the Lynna City shop (you then find him lost in the Fairies' Woods).
RUNES.what: A friendly Dodongo who swims — including against strong currents — and can eat enemies and cross water safely.
RUNES.tip: Dimitri is the water specialist; he ignores currents that would sweep Link away.

RUNES.name: Moosh
RUNES.from: Your companion if you get no flute first and visit the Nuun Highlands.
RUNES.what: A winged blue bear. Ride him to flap and hover/glide across wide gaps, and ground-pound enemies.
RUNES.tip: Hold the jump to flutter and float over pits too wide to jump normally.

RUNES.name: Gasha Seed
RUNES.from: Found in chests, given by NPCs, and earned across the adventure; plant them in soft Gasha-Spot soil.
RUNES.what: Plant one in a Gasha patch, then walk past it many times — it grows into a Gasha Tree that drops a Gasha Nut full of rupees, hearts, rings, or rare items.
RUNES.tip: Plant them early and keep wandering; the nut ripens as you rack up steps and battles nearby.

RUNES.name: Magic Rings
RUNES.from: Found all over Labrynna (chests, Gasha Nuts, NPCs, prizes) — but they must be appraised by Vasu before you can use them.
RUNES.what: Equippable passive boosters. Each ring grants a different effect (extra damage, defense, special abilities, fun gimmicks). You equip them via the Ring Box.
RUNES.tip: Take EVERY ring to Vasu to identify it — unappraised rings do nothing and you won't know what they are.

RUNES.name: Ring Box
RUNES.from: Given by Vasu when you first visit his ring shop; upgraded boxes (holding more rings at once) come later.
RUNES.what: Stores your Magic Rings and sets how many you can have equipped at the same time.
RUNES.tip: A bigger Ring Box lets you stack multiple ring effects — chase the upgrades to wear more at once.

RUNES.name: Vasu's Ring Appraisal
RUNES.from: Vasu runs his ring shop in Lynna City; bring him every unidentified ring.
RUNES.what: Vasu reveals what an unappraised ring actually does, after which you can equip it from your Ring Box.
RUNES.tip: This isn't an item you carry — it's the service that makes all your found rings usable. Visit often.

RUNES.name: Bottle
RUNES.from: A few are hidden across Labrynna (from NPCs, trades, and secrets).
RUNES.what: An empty container that holds Magic Potion, fairies, water, or other liquids you collect.
RUNES.tip: Catch a fairy in an empty bottle — it auto-revives you when you fall in battle.

RUNES.name: Magic Potion
RUNES.from: Bought from / given by NPCs (including Maple and the potion shop) and stored in a Bottle.
RUNES.what: A healing brew kept in a Bottle. When your hearts hit zero it's used automatically to fully refill them, saving you from a game over.
RUNES.tip: Always travel with a bottled potion before a boss — it's a free second life.

CHAMPIONS.name: Eternal Spirit
CHAMPIONS.note: The first Essence of Time, won from Spirit's Grave.

CHAMPIONS.name: Ancient Wood
CHAMPIONS.note: The second Essence, won from the Wing Dungeon.

CHAMPIONS.name: Echoing Howl
CHAMPIONS.note: The third Essence, won from the Moonlit Grotto (boss Shadow Hag).

CHAMPIONS.name: Burning Flame
CHAMPIONS.note: The fourth Essence, won from the Skull Dungeon (boss Eyesoar).

CHAMPIONS.name: Sacred Soil
CHAMPIONS.note: The fifth Essence, won from the Crown Dungeon (boss Smog).

CHAMPIONS.name: Lonely Peak
CHAMPIONS.note: The sixth Essence, won from Mermaid's Cave (boss Octogon).

CHAMPIONS.name: Rolling Sea
CHAMPIONS.note: The seventh Essence, won from Jabu-Jabu's Belly (boss Plasmarine).

CHAMPIONS.name: Falling Star
CHAMPIONS.note: The eighth and last Essence, won from the Ancient Tomb — completing the Maku Seed.

ROADMAP.name: The Eight Essences of Time
ROADMAP.note: One Essence waits in each of Labrynna's eight dungeons. Gather all eight and the Maku Tree forms the Maku Seed, your key to Veran's seat of power.

ROADMAP.name: The Harp of Ages
ROADMAP.note: The Harp lets you cross between the past and the present at time portals. Reshape history — what you do long ago changes the world today.

ROADMAP.name: Magic Rings
ROADMAP.note: Find ring boxes and Gasha nuts, then have Vasu appraise them. Equip rings for powers. There are 64 in all (shareable with a linked Oracle of Seasons game).

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: Twelve are hidden across Labrynna — some only reachable in one era.

TIPS.name: Traveling through time
items: The Harp of Ages moves you between the PAST and the PRESENT at time portals. The Tune of Echoes opens portals; the Tune of Currents and Tune of Ages warp you between eras directly once learned.

Cause and effect across time is the whole game: block a river in the past and a lake is dry in the present; plant a seed long ago and a tree stands today. When stuck, ask 'what could I change in the OTHER era?'
Some screens exist only in one era, or are blocked in one and open in the other — flip time and look again.

TIPS.name: Seeds, hooks & tools
items: Magic Seeds power the Seed Shooter and tools: Ember (fire), Scent (lure/damage), Pegasus (speed), Gale (warp), Mystery (random). Refill from seed trees.
The Switch Hook swaps Link's place with a block or enemy — cross gaps and reposition things; it later becomes the Long Hook with longer reach.
Gasha Seeds planted in soft soil grow Gasha Nuts holding rings, rupees, and items.

TIPS.name: Rings & the linked game
items: Collect ring boxes and have Vasu (in Lynna) appraise unidentified rings; equip one for effects (Power Ring boosts attack, Armor Ring defense, and dozens more).
Oracle of Ages and Oracle of Seasons are a linked pair: beat one, then enter its password into the other for a harder 'linked' quest, shared rings, and the TRUE final boss (Twinrova → Ganon).
Ages leans more on puzzles than combat — take your time reading each room; a bottled Fairy helps in the harder dungeons.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Pieces of Heart
COLLECTIBLES.note: Every 4 make a full Heart Container — some only reachable in one era.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Magic Rings
COLLECTIBLES.note: Appraised by Vasu; equip one for a power. 64 in all (shareable with a linked Oracle of Seasons).
